title: Modernize linter rules: add sloglint/unparam, drop gocritic, refine exclusions
description:
## Description
With the v2 config format in place, modernize the linter rule set:
- Add sloglint (structured logging consistency) and unparam (unused parameters)
- Remove gocritic (too opinionated)
- Switch gosec from blanket to path-specific exclusions
- Expand errcheck exclusions for pragmatic noise reduction

## Instructions

### Step 1: Add sloglint linter

Add `sloglint` to the `linters.enable` list. This enforces consistent usage of
Go's `log/slog` structured logging. We have ~199 slog call sites across 5 packages.

No special settings needed initially. If it produces too many findings about key
naming, consider adding:
```yaml
sloglint:
  key-naming-case: snake
```

### Step 2: Add unparam linter

Add `unparam` to the `linters.enable` list. This detects unused function parameters.

If it flags interface implementations or params kept for API symmetry, add targeted
exclusions like beads does:
```yaml
exclusions:
  rules:
    - path: 'specific/file\.go'
      linters: [unparam]
      text: 'functionName.*paramName'
```

### Step 3: Remove gocritic

Remove `gocritic` from the `linters.enable` list. Also remove any gocritic-specific
settings if present (there are none currently).

### Step 4: Switch gosec to path-specific exclusions

Remove the blanket gosec excludes:
```yaml
# REMOVE THIS from settings:
gosec:
  excludes:
    - G304
    - G301
    - G306
```

Replace with path-specific exclusion rules. Since this is a file management CLI
where ALL file operations use internally-constructed paths (verified: 30 production
files, all safe), use path-pattern exclusions:

```yaml
exclusions:
  rules:
    # G304/G301/G306: aimgr is a file management tool. All file operations use
    # paths constructed internally from repo path, XDG directories, or tool
    # directory info — never from arbitrary user input.
    - path: '^pkg/'
      linters: [gosec]
      text: 'G304|G301|G306'
    - path: '^cmd/'
      linters: [gosec]
      text: 'G304|G301|G306'

    # Keep existing: G204 in tests
    - path: '_test\.go'
      linters: [gosec]
      text: 'G204'
```

This is more surgical than blanket excludes because:
- New top-level directories would NOT be excluded (forces conscious decision)
- The intent is documented in comments
- It's explicit about which directories are trusted for file operations

### Step 5: Expand errcheck exclusions

Expand the errcheck exclude-functions list. Add functions where error returns are
rarely actionable (following beads' pragmatic approach):

```yaml
errcheck:
  exclude-functions:
    # Existing
    - (io.Closer).Close
    - (*os.File).Close
    # New: cleanup operations where errors are non-critical
    - (os).RemoveAll
    - (os).Remove
    - (os).MkdirAll       # Often best-effort, checked by subsequent operations
    # New: environment operations in tests/setup
    - (os).Setenv
    - (os).Unsetenv
    - (os).Chdir
    # New: fmt write operations (CLI output, rarely fails)
    - (fmt).Fprintf
    - (fmt).Fprintln
    - fmt.Fprintf
    - fmt.Fprintln
```

### Step 6: Update linter count in comments

The linter list should now be: govet, staticcheck, errcheck, gosec, dupl, gocyclo,
goconst, misspell, unconvert, sloglint, unparam (11 linters, was 10).

## Files to Modify
- .golangci.yml (all changes in this one file)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] sloglint enabled and configured
- [ ] unparam enabled (with targeted exclusions if needed)
- [ ] gocritic removed from enable list
- [ ] gosec no longer has blanket G304/G301/G306 excludes
- [ ] gosec has path-specific exclusions for pkg/ and cmd/
- [ ] errcheck has expanded exclude-functions list
- [ ] `golangci-lint run` completes without config errors
- [ ] Total enabled linters: 11

## Notes
- Run `golangci-lint run --timeout=5m` after each step to catch issues early
- sloglint may surface many findings if slog usage is inconsistent — that's expected
  and will be fixed in the next task
- unparam findings in interface implementations should be excluded, not fixed
close_reason: Linter rules modernized: added sloglint/unparam, removed gocritic, path-specific gosec, expanded errcheck

title: Upgrade golangci-lint to v2 and modernize linter configuration
description:
## Description
Upgrade golangci-lint from v1 to v2 config format and modernize the linter rule set.
This fixes the CI bug (golangci-lint built with Go 1.24 can't lint Go 1.25.6 code) and
aligns our linting approach with modern Go best practices, informed by the beads project.

## Goals
- Fix broken CI lint step (resolves ai-config-manager-k6o8)
- Migrate .golangci.yml to v2 config schema
- Update CI workflow to use golangci-lint v2 (golangci-lint-action@v7)
- Add sloglint + unparam linters (from beads project)
- Drop gocritic (too opinionated for this codebase)
- Switch gosec from blanket exclusions to path-specific exclusions
- Expand errcheck exclusions to reduce false positive noise
- Fix all lint violations introduced by the changes

## Decisions Made
- Keep dupl + goconst (they catch concrete issues)
- Drop gocritic (opinionated, beads doesn't use it either)
- Path-specific gosec exclusions (surgical, not blanket)
- Pragmatic errcheck exclusions (os.RemoveAll, os.MkdirAll, fmt.Fprintf etc.)
- Keep linting test files with exclusions (don't skip entirely)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] CI lint step passes on Go 1.25.6
- [ ] .golangci.yml uses v2 config schema
- [ ] sloglint and unparam linters enabled and passing
- [ ] gocritic removed
- [ ] gosec uses path-specific exclusions
- [ ] errcheck has expanded pragmatic exclusions
- [ ] Zero new lint violations
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
close_reason: All tasks and gate complete. golangci-lint upgraded to v2, CI fixed, 11 linters configured (added sloglint/unparam, removed gocritic), path-specific gosec, expanded errcheck. Zero violations, all tests pass.

title: Uninstall command has same multi-package bug as install
description:
## Description
The `uninstall` command has the exact same bug as `install` - packages only work when passed as a single argument.

## Steps to Reproduce
```bash
# This fails:
aimgr uninstall skill/pptx package/opencode-coder
# Error: invalid resource type 'package': must be one of 'skill', 'command', or 'agent'

# But this works:
aimgr uninstall package/opencode-coder
```

## Root Cause
Same pattern as install bug - lines 96-101 in `cmd/uninstall.go`:
```go
if len(args) == 1 && strings.HasPrefix(args[0], "package/") {
    // Only triggers for single package argument
}
```

## Fix Required
Apply same fix as install.go - separate packages from resources before processing and route packages through `uninstallPackage()`.

## Related
- Fixed in install: ai-config-manager-jam1

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `aimgr uninstall skill/x package/y` works correctly
- [ ] `aimgr uninstall package/x package/y` works correctly
- [ ] Existing single-package uninstall still works
close_reason: Fixed - same pattern as install fix applied to uninstall.go

title: Add unit tests for multi-resource command scenarios
description:

## Instructions

2. Add tests for each command that accepts multiple resources
3. Test scenarios:
   - Multiple resources of same type
   - Multiple resources of different types
   - Mix of resources and packages
   - Multiple packages
   - Patterns mixed with explicit resources

## Test Cases Required

### Install Command
- [ ] `install skill/a skill/b` - multiple skills
- [ ] `install skill/a command/b` - mixed types
- [ ] `install skill/a package/b` - skill + package
- [ ] `install package/a package/b` - multiple packages
- [ ] `install package/a skill/b command/c` - package + mixed types

### Uninstall Command
- [ ] Same scenarios as install

### Any Other Multi-Resource Commands
- [ ] Identify and test similarly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All multi-resource scenarios have unit tests
- [ ] Tests catch the original bug (would have failed before fix)
- [ ] Tests pass after fix
close_reason: Added 11 tests covering multi-resource scenarios for install and uninstall

title: Package install fails when mixed with other resources
description:
## Description
When installing a package together with other resources, the package installation fails with "not found" error, even though installing the package alone works fine.

## Steps to Reproduce
```bash
# This fails:
aimgr install skill/pptx skill/ai-resource-manager package/opencode-coder
# Error: package 'opencode-coder' not found in repository

# But this works:
aimgr install package/opencode-coder
```

## Root Cause
In `cmd/install.go`, there are TWO separate code paths for handling packages:

1. **Single package argument (lines 131-177)** - Works correctly:
```go
if len(args) == 1 && strings.HasPrefix(args[0], "package/") {
    // Calls installPackage() directly - WORKS
}
```

2. **Multiple arguments (lines 223-253)** - Broken:
```go
for _, arg := range args {
    matches, err := ExpandPattern(manager, arg)  // Returns package/x as-is
    expandedArgs = append(expandedArgs, matches...)
}
for _, arg := range expandedArgs {
    result := processInstall(arg, installer, manager)  // Doesn't handle packages!
}
```

The `processInstall()` function tries to look up packages as resources via `manager.Get()`, but packages aren't resources - they're a separate entity type.

## Impact
- Users cannot install packages alongside other resources
- Inconsistent behavior between single and multi-resource installs
- Poor UX - users expect consistent behavior

## Fix Required
The multi-argument code path must detect `package/` prefixes and route them to `installPackage()` instead of `processInstall()`, similar to how `installFromManifest()` handles it (lines 366-397).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `aimgr install skill/x package/y` works correctly

title: Add modifications generator package
description:
## Description

Create the package responsible for generating tool-specific file variants in the `.modifications/` folder. This is the core transformation logic.

## Instructions

1. Create new file `pkg/modifications/generator.go`

2. Implement the Generator:
```go
package modifications

import (
    "github.com/hkford/ai-config-manager/pkg/config"
    "github.com/hkford/ai-config-manager/pkg/resource"
)

// Generator handles creation of tool-specific file modifications
type Generator struct {
    repoPath string
    mappings config.TypeMappings
    logger   *slog.Logger
}

// NewGenerator creates a generator for the given repo
func NewGenerator(repoPath string, mappings config.TypeMappings) *Generator

// ModificationsDir returns the .modifications directory path
func (g *Generator) ModificationsDir() string

// GenerateForResource creates tool-specific variants for a resource
// Returns list of tools that had modifications generated
func (g *Generator) GenerateForResource(res *resource.Resource) ([]string, error)

// GenerateAll regenerates all modifications for all resources
func (g *Generator) GenerateAll() error

// CleanupForResource removes modifications for a resource
func (g *Generator) CleanupForResource(res *resource.Resource) error

// CleanupAll removes all modifications
func (g *Generator) CleanupAll() error

// GetModificationPath returns path to modified file for a tool
// Returns empty string if no modification exists
func (g *Generator) GetModificationPath(res *resource.Resource, tool string) string
```

3. Generation logic for `GenerateForResource`:
```
For each tool with mappings:
    1. Determine which files to process:
       - skill: only SKILL.md in the resource directory
       - agent: all .md files in resource path
       - command: all .md files in resource path
    2. For each file:
       a. Read and parse frontmatter
       b. For each field in mappings for this resource type:
          - Get current value (or empty for null mapping)
          - Look up tool-specific mapping
          - If mapping exists, update field
       c. If any fields changed:
          - Create output dir: .modifications/<tool>/<type>s/<name>/
          - Write transformed file
```

4. Directory structure:
```
.modifications/
  opencode/
    skills/
      my-skill/
        SKILL.md
    agents/
      reviewer.md
    commands/
      deploy.md
  claude/
    skills/
      my-skill/
        SKILL.md
```

5. Add helper function for install package:
```go
// HasModification checks if a modification exists for resource+tool
func (g *Generator) HasModification(res *resource.Resource, tool string) bool
```

## Files to Create

- `pkg/modifications/generator.go` - Main implementation
- `pkg/modifications/generator_test.go` - Unit tests

## Dependencies

- Depends on: ai-config-manager-mxdg.2 (config schema)
- Depends on: ai-config-manager-mxdg.3 (frontmatter parser)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GenerateForResource creates correct directory structure
- [ ] Only processes relevant files per resource type (SKILL.md for skills, all .md for agents/commands)
- [ ] Frontmatter fields are correctly transformed
- [ ] Null mapping works (adds field when missing)
- [ ] Files without matching mappings are skipped (no modification created)
- [ ] CleanupForResource removes modifications correctly
- [ ] GetModificationPath returns correct path when exists, empty when not
- [ ] Unit tests cover main scenarios

## Test Scenarios

1. Skill with model field + opencode mapping -> creates .modifications/opencode/skills/...
2. Skill with model field, no claude mapping -> no .modifications/claude/...
3. Skill without model field + null mapping -> creates modification with field added
4. Agent with multiple .md files -> all files processed
5. Resource type without any mappings -> no modifications created
close_reason: Implemented modifications generator package with GenerateForResource, GenerateAll, CleanupForResource, CleanupAll, GetModificationPath, and HasModification functions. Package correctly handles skills (directory with SKILL.md), agents (.md files), and commands (.md files in commands/ directory). Includes 22 comprehensive unit tests covering all scenarios: model mappings, null mappings (adds field when missing), multiple tools, cleanup operations, and edge cases.

title: Add frontmatter parser utility
description:
## Description

Create a utility to parse and modify YAML frontmatter in markdown files. This is needed to read field values and generate transformed variants.

## Instructions

1. Create new file `pkg/frontmatter/frontmatter.go`

2. Implement the Frontmatter struct and parser:
```go
package frontmatter

// Frontmatter represents parsed YAML frontmatter
type Frontmatter struct {
    Fields  map[string]interface{}  // Parsed YAML fields
    Content string                   // Markdown content after frontmatter
    Raw     string                   // Original frontmatter YAML string
}

// Parse extracts frontmatter from markdown content
// Returns nil Frontmatter (not error) if no frontmatter present
func Parse(content []byte) (*Frontmatter, error)

// HasFrontmatter checks if content has YAML frontmatter
func HasFrontmatter(content []byte) bool

// GetString returns a string field value, empty string if not found
func (f *Frontmatter) GetString(key string) string

// SetField sets a field value in the frontmatter
func (f *Frontmatter) SetField(key string, value interface{})

// Render produces the full markdown with updated frontmatter
func (f *Frontmatter) Render() []byte
```

3. Frontmatter format to support:
```markdown
---
model: sonnet-4.5
temperature: 0.7
custom_field: value
---
# Content here
```

4. Edge cases to handle:
   - No frontmatter (content starts without `---`)
   - Empty frontmatter (`---\n---`)
   - Frontmatter with complex YAML (nested objects, arrays)
   - Content with `---` later in the document (only first block is frontmatter)

5. Add unit tests in `pkg/frontmatter/frontmatter_test.go`:
   - Parse valid frontmatter
   - Parse content without frontmatter
   - Parse empty frontmatter
   - GetString for existing and missing keys
   - SetField and Render roundtrip
   - Content preservation (no modifications to body)

## Files to Create

- `pkg/frontmatter/frontmatter.go` - Main implementation
- `pkg/frontmatter/frontmatter_test.go` - Unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Parse extracts frontmatter fields correctly
- [ ] Parse returns nil (not error) for no frontmatter
- [ ] GetString returns empty string for missing keys
- [ ] SetField + Render produces valid markdown
- [ ] Original content body is preserved exactly
- [ ] Handles edge cases (empty frontmatter, no frontmatter)
- [ ] All unit tests pass

## Notes

Use `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` for YAML parsing (already a dependency).
close_reason: Implemented frontmatter parser with Parse, HasFrontmatter, GetString, SetField, and Render functions. All 7 test functions pass covering valid/invalid frontmatter, edge cases (empty, no frontmatter, CRLF), roundtrip, and content preservation.

title: Add mappings config schema
description:
## Description

Extend the config schema to support the new `mappings` section for tool-specific field transformations.

## Instructions

1. Open `pkg/config/config.go`

2. Add new types for the mappings structure:
```go
// FieldMappings maps logical values to tool-specific values
// Structure: field_name -> logical_value -> tool_name -> actual_value
// Special key "null" maps missing/empty values
type FieldMappings map[string]map[string]map[string]string

// TypeMappings holds field mappings for each resource type
type TypeMappings struct {
    Skill   FieldMappings `yaml:"skill,omitempty"`
    Agent   FieldMappings `yaml:"agent,omitempty"`
    Command FieldMappings `yaml:"command,omitempty"`
}
```

3. Add `Mappings` field to the main `Config` struct:
```go
type Config struct {
    Install  InstallConfig `yaml:"install"`
    Repo     RepoConfig    `yaml:"repo"`
    Mappings TypeMappings  `yaml:"mappings,omitempty"`  // NEW
}
```

4. Add helper methods to TypeMappings:
```go
// GetMapping returns the tool-specific value for a field
// Returns (mappedValue, true) if mapping exists
// Returns ("", false) if no mapping exists
func (tm *TypeMappings) GetMapping(resourceType resource.ResourceType, fieldName string, logicalValue string, toolName string) (string, bool)

// GetMappingWithNull handles null mapping for missing values
// If logicalValue is empty, checks for "null" key mapping
func (tm *TypeMappings) GetMappingWithNull(resourceType resource.ResourceType, fieldName string, logicalValue string, toolName string) (string, bool)

// GetToolsWithMappings returns list of tools that have any mappings defined
func (tm *TypeMappings) GetToolsWithMappings() []string
```

5. Add validation in `Config.Validate()`:
   - Validate tool names in mappings match known tools (claude, opencode, copilot, windsurf)
   - Log warning for unknown tool names (don't error, allow future tools)

## Files to Modify

- `pkg/config/config.go` - Add types and methods

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config can be loaded with mappings section
- [ ] Empty mappings section works (backwards compatible)
- [ ] GetMapping returns correct tool-specific values
- [ ] GetMappingWithNull handles empty/missing values
- [ ] GetToolsWithMappings returns only tools with actual mappings
- [ ] Validation warns on unknown tool names
- [ ] Existing config tests still pass

## Test Cases

```yaml
# Test config
mappings:
  skill:
    model:
      sonnet-4.5:
        opencode: "langdock/claude-sonnet-4-5"
        claude: "claude-sonnet-4"
      null:
        opencode: "langdock/default-model"
  agent:
    model:
      gpt-4:
        opencode: "langdock/gpt-4"
```

Expected:
- GetMapping(Skill, "model", "sonnet-4.5", "opencode") -> "langdock/claude-sonnet-4-5", true
- GetMapping(Skill, "model", "sonnet-4.5", "windsurf") -> "", false
- GetMappingWithNull(Skill, "model", "", "opencode") -> "langdock/default-model", true
- GetToolsWithMappings() -> ["opencode", "claude"]
close_reason: Implemented mappings config schema with TypeMappings struct and helper methods (GetMapping, GetMappingWithNull, GetToolsWithMappings, HasAny). Added validation that warns on unknown tool names. All tests pass.

title: Tool-Specific Field Mappings
description:
## Description

Implement tool-specific field transformations for AI resources. Different AI tools (Claude, OpenCode, Windsurf, Copilot) require different values for the same frontmatter fields (e.g., model names). This feature allows users to define logical field values that get mapped to tool-specific values.

## Problem

- OpenCode requires models in `provider/model` format (e.g., `langdock/claude-sonnet-4-5`)
- Claude uses just model names (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4`)
- Current symlink architecture can't handle per-tool variations
- Same applies to any frontmatter field, not just models

## Solution

1. Add `mappings` config section for field value transformations
2. Generate tool-specific file variants in `.modifications/` folder during repo sync/add
3. Install logic checks for modification files and symlinks to them when present

## Repository Layout

```
repo/
  skills/my-skill/SKILL.md          # Original (untouched)
  agents/reviewer.md                # Original (untouched)
  .metadata/skills/my-skill.yaml    # Existing metadata
  .modifications/                   # NEW: generated variants
    opencode/
      skills/my-skill/SKILL.md      # Transformed for opencode
      agents/reviewer.md
    claude/
      skills/my-skill/SKILL.md      # Transformed for claude
      agents/reviewer.md
```

## Config Schema

```yaml
# ~/.config/aimgr/aimgr.yaml
mappings:
  skill:
    model:
      sonnet-4.5:
        opencode: "langdock/claude-sonnet-4-5"
        claude: "claude-sonnet-4"
      null:  # Maps missing/empty field to tool-specific value
        opencode: "langdock/default-model"
  agent:
    model:
      # Same structure, applies to all .md files in agents/
  command:
    model:
      # Same structure, applies to all .md files in commands/
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Original files untouched** - Remote content stays pristine for debugging
2. **Symlinks preserved** - Install symlinks to `.modifications/` when present, else original
3. **Upfront generation** - Variants created during sync/add, not install
4. **Per-type mappings** - skill/agent/command have separate mapping sections
5. **Null handling** - Can map missing/empty values to tool-specific defaults
6. **File scope**: 
   - skill: Only SKILL.md
   - agent: All .md files
   - command: All .md files

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Config schema supports `mappings` section with skill/agent/command types
- [ ] Frontmatter parsing works for all markdown resources
- [ ] `.modifications/` folder generated during repo add/sync/remove
- [ ] Install prefers `.modifications/` variant when present
- [ ] Backwards compatible (no mappings = current behavior)
- [ ] `null` key maps missing values to tool-specific defaults
close_reason: Epic complete: All success criteria met - mappings config schema added (pkg/config), frontmatter parser implemented (pkg/frontmatter), modifications generator created (pkg/modifications), integrated into repo add/sync/remove operations, install uses modifications when present, backwards compatible, null value mapping works. All 9 subtasks completed and verified.

(3) aimgr list prints '⚠ N broken resource(s) found' warning to stderr. (4) aimgr verify --fix reinstalls resources that exist in repo ('✓ Reinstalled skill/fix-skill'). (5) aimgr verify --fix shows clear message for unrecoverable resources ('✗ Removed broken symlink. Resource no longer exists in repository'). (6) JSON/YAML output includes health field ('health: broken'/'health: ok'). (7) All tests pass: make test (unit + integration + E2E, including 7 new broken symlink tests in list_broken_test.go). (8) No lint errors: make vet clean. E2E lifecycle validated: install → repo delete → detect broken → fix → verify healthy.

title: Make aimgr verify --fix actually reinstall recoverable resources
description:
## Description

`aimgr verify --fix` currently removes broken symlinks but does NOT reinstall the resource. It has two TODO comments (lines 466, 474 in `cmd/project_verify.go`) and the `manager` parameter is typed as `interface{}` making it impossible to call install functions. This task completes the fix workflow so broken resources that still exist in the repo are automatically reinstalled.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Change the `manager` parameter type

In `cmd/project_verify.go`, `fixVerifyIssues()` at line 449:

Change:
```go
func fixVerifyIssues(projectPath string, issues []VerifyIssue, manager interface{}) error {
```
To:
```go
func fixVerifyIssues(projectPath string, issues []VerifyIssue, repoManager *repo.Manager) error {
```

Add the necessary import for the repo package. Also update the caller at line 104 to pass the manager with the correct type (it's already a `*repo.Manager` from `NewManagerWithLogLevel()` at line 49, just needs to be passed correctly).

### Step 2: Implement reinstallation for broken/wrong-repo symlinks

Replace the TODO at line 466. In the `"broken"`, `"wrong-repo"` case (lines 455-469):

```go
case "broken", "wrong-repo":
    fmt.Printf("  Fixing %s...\n", issue.Resource)
    
    // Remove broken symlink
    if err := os.Remove(issue.Path); err != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Failed to remove: %v\n", err)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    // Determine resource type and name from the issue
    resType, resName := parseResourceFromIssue(issue)
    
    // Check if resource still exists in repo
    _, err := repoManager.Get(resName, resType)
    if err != nil {
        // Resource no longer in repo — can't reinstall
        fmt.Printf("    ✗ Removed broken symlink. Resource '%s' no longer exists in repository.\n", resName)
        fmt.Printf("      Consider removing from %s: aimgr uninstall %s/%s\n", manifest.ManifestFileName, resType, resName)
        fixed++
        continue
    }
    
    // Reinstall using the installer
    detectedTools, err := tools.DetectExistingTools(projectPath)
    if err != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Failed to detect tools: %v\n", err)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    installer := install.NewInstallerWithTargets(projectPath, detectedTools)
    var installErr error
    switch resType {
    case resource.Command:
        installErr = installer.InstallCommand(resName, repoManager)
    case resource.Skill:
        installErr = installer.InstallSkill(resName, repoManager)
    case resource.Agent:
        installErr = installer.InstallAgent(resName, repoManager)
    }
    
    if installErr != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Failed to reinstall: %v\n", installErr)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("    ✓ Reinstalled %s/%s\n", resType, resName)
    fixed++
```

### Step 3: Implement reinstallation for not-installed resources

Replace the TODO at line 474. In the `"not-installed"` case (lines 471-475):

```go
case "not-installed":
    fmt.Printf("  Installing %s...\n", issue.Resource)
    
    // Parse "type/name" from the resource reference
    parts := strings.SplitN(issue.Resource, "/", 2)
    if len(parts) != 2 {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Cannot parse resource reference: %s\n", issue.Resource)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    resType := resource.ParseType(parts[0])
    resName := parts[1]
    
    // Check if resource exists in repo
    _, err := repoManager.Get(resName, resType)
    if err != nil {
        fmt.Printf("    ✗ Resource '%s' not found in repository. Remove from %s or run 'aimgr repo add' to add it.\n", 
            issue.Resource, manifest.ManifestFileName)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    // Install it
    detectedTools, err := tools.DetectExistingTools(projectPath)
    if err != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Failed to detect tools: %v\n", err)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    installer := install.NewInstallerWithTargets(projectPath, detectedTools)
    var installErr error
    switch resType {
    case resource.Command:
        installErr = installer.InstallCommand(resName, repoManager)
    case resource.Skill:
        installErr = installer.InstallSkill(resName, repoManager)
    case resource.Agent:
        installErr = installer.InstallAgent(resName, repoManager)
    }
    
    if installErr != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "    Failed to install: %v\n", installErr)
        failed++
        continue
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("    ✓ Installed %s\n", issue.Resource)
    fixed++
```

### Step 4: Add `parseResourceFromIssue()` helper

Create a helper function to extract resource type and name from a `VerifyIssue`:

```go
func parseResourceFromIssue(issue VerifyIssue) (resource.ResourceType, string) {
    // Issue.Path is like /project/.opencode/skills/my-skill or /project/.claude/commands/my-cmd.md
    // Issue.Resource is the entry name (e.g., "my-skill", "my-cmd.md")
    
    name := issue.Resource
    
    // Determine type from the directory path
    pathLower := strings.ToLower(issue.Path)
    switch {
    case strings.Contains(pathLower, "/commands/"):
        name = strings.TrimSuffix(name, ".md")
        return resource.Command, name
    case strings.Contains(pathLower, "/skills/"):
        return resource.Skill, name
    case strings.Contains(pathLower, "/agents/"):
        name = strings.TrimSuffix(name, ".md")
        return resource.Agent, name
    default:
        // Fallback — try to infer from name
        return resource.Skill, name
    }
}
```

### Step 5: Update the success message

At the end of `fixVerifyIssues()` (around line 490), update the message to NOT say "Run 'aimgr install' to complete repairs" since we now do the reinstall:

```go
if fixed > 0 {
    fmt.Printf("✓ Fixed %d issue(s)\n", fixed)
}
```

Remove the old `fmt.Println("Run 'aimgr install' to complete repairs")` line.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/project_verify.go` — Change `fixVerifyIssues()` signature, implement reinstallation for broken/wrong-repo/not-installed cases, add `parseResourceFromIssue()` helper, update success messaging

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `fixVerifyIssues()` accepts `*repo.Manager` instead of `interface{}`
- [ ] Broken symlinks are removed AND resource is reinstalled if it exists in repo
- [ ] Resources not in repo get clear "not found in repository" messaging
- [ ] Not-installed resources (manifest-only) are installed automatically
- [ ] Success/failure counts are accurate
- [ ] No more TODO comments in `fixVerifyIssues()`
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] No new lint errors: `make vet`

## Notes

- `resource.ParseType()` may not exist — check and use the appropriate function. It might be a switch on string like `"skill"`, `"command"`, `"agent"`.
- The `repoManager.Get()` function returns the resource if it exists. Use this to check recoverability.
- The `install.NewInstallerWithTargets()` is the same constructor used in `cmd/install.go`.
- Be careful with the tool detection — use `tools.DetectExistingTools()` which looks at what's already in the project.
close_reason: Implemented full reinstallation in fixVerifyIssues(): changed parameter from interface{} to *repo.Manager, broken/wrong-repo symlinks are now removed and reinstalled via install.NewInstallerWithTargets(), not-installed resources from manifest are automatically installed, added parseResourceFromIssue() helper to extract resource type from directory paths, removed old TODO comments and 'Run aimgr install' fallback messaging. All tests pass, no lint errors.

title: Show broken resources in aimgr list with health indicator
close_reason: Implemented health indicator in aimgr list: added Health field to ResourceInfo struct, propagated health from resource.Resource in buildResourceInfo(), added Status column to table (✓ for healthy, ✗ broken for broken), added broken resource warning to stderr, and health field is included in JSON/YAML output. All tests pass, no lint errors.

title: Return broken resources from List() with health status
description:
## Description

The `List()` function in `pkg/install/installer.go` (lines 411-558) silently skips broken symlinks via `continue` statements. This is the root cause of the entire problem — downstream code never learns that broken resources exist. We need `List()` to return broken resources alongside healthy ones, with a status indicator.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Add a `Health` field to `resource.Resource`

In `pkg/resource/resource.go`, add a `Health` field to the `Resource` struct:

```go
type ResourceHealth string

const (
    HealthOK     ResourceHealth = "ok"
    HealthBroken ResourceHealth = "broken"  // symlink target doesn't exist
)
```

Add to the `Resource` struct:
```go
Health ResourceHealth `json:"health,omitempty" yaml:"health,omitempty"`
```

### Step 2: Modify `List()` to capture broken symlinks

In `pkg/install/installer.go`, modify `List()`. Currently at lines 450-454 (commands), 490-493 (skills), and 538-542 (agents), when `resource.LoadCommand/Skill/Agent(target)` fails or `os.Stat(target)` fails, the code does `continue`. Instead:

**For commands** (around line 444-454):
```go
// After os.Readlink succeeds (line 445), before LoadCommand:
// Check if target exists
if _, err := os.Stat(target); err != nil {
    // Broken symlink — create a minimal resource entry
    name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md")
    resourceMap[name] = resource.Resource{
        Name:   name,
        Type:   resource.Command,
        Path:   target,
        Health: resource.HealthBroken,
    }
    continue
}
// Then proceed with LoadCommand as before
res, err := resource.LoadCommand(target)
if err != nil {
    continue
}
res.Health = resource.HealthOK
resourceMap[res.Name] = *res
```

**For skills** (around line 484-500):
```go
// After os.Readlink succeeds, check target:
targetInfo, err := os.Stat(target)
if err != nil || !targetInfo.IsDir() {
    // Broken symlink
    resourceMap[entry.Name()] = resource.Resource{
        Name:   entry.Name(),
        Type:   resource.Skill,
        Path:   target,
        Health: resource.HealthBroken,
    }
    continue
}
res, err := resource.LoadSkill(target)
if err != nil {
    continue
}
res.Health = resource.HealthOK
resourceMap[res.Name] = *res
```

**For agents** (around line 532-542):
```go
// Same pattern as commands
if _, err := os.Stat(target); err != nil {
    name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".md")
    resourceMap[name] = resource.Resource{
        Name:   name,
        Type:   resource.Agent,
        Path:   target,
        Health: resource.HealthBroken,
    }
    continue
}
res, err := resource.LoadAgent(target)
if err != nil {
    continue
}
res.Health = resource.HealthOK
resourceMap[res.Name] = *res
```

### Step 3: Ensure existing callers are unaffected

The `Health` field defaults to the zero value (`""`). Callers that don't check it will behave exactly as before. But to be safe, search for all callers of `List()` and verify they handle the new field gracefully:

- `cmd/list_installed.go` line 119 — will be updated in the next task
- `cmd/clean.go` — check if it calls `List()` and whether broken resources would cause issues
- Any other callers

For `IsInstalled()` (line 561): This should continue to return `false` for broken symlinks (current behavior via `os.Stat` at line 588). No changes needed here.

## Files to Modify

- `pkg/resource/resource.go` — Add `ResourceHealth` type and `Health` field to `Resource` struct
- `pkg/install/installer.go` — Modify `List()` to capture broken symlinks with `HealthBroken` status, set `HealthOK` for valid resources

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ResourceHealth` type with `HealthOK` and `HealthBroken` constants exists
- [ ] `Resource` struct has `Health` field with json/yaml tags
- [ ] `List()` returns broken-symlink resources with `Health == HealthBroken`
- [ ] `List()` returns valid resources with `Health == HealthOK`
- [ ] Broken resources have `Name`, `Type`, `Path` populated correctly
- [ ] `IsInstalled()` behavior unchanged (returns false for broken symlinks)
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] No new lint errors: `make vet`

## Notes

- The `Name` for broken symlinks is derived from the symlink filename (strip `.md` for commands/agents, use directory name for skills) since we can't load the resource's frontmatter.
- The `Health` field uses `omitempty` so existing JSON/YAML output isn't affected for healthy resources.
- This is a foundational change — Task 2 depends on this to display broken resources in `aimgr list`.
close_reason: Added ResourceHealth type (HealthOK, HealthBroken) and Health field to Resource struct in pkg/resource/types.go. Modified List() in pkg/install/installer.go to return broken-symlink resources with HealthBroken status instead of silently skipping them, and set HealthOK on successfully loaded resources. IsInstalled() behavior unchanged. All tests pass, no lint errors.

title: Resilient Installed Resources — Detect and Recover from Broken Symlinks
description:
## Description

All `aimgr install` operations create symlinks from project tool directories (`.claude/`, `.opencode/`, etc.) into the global repo. When the underlying repo resource is deleted — via `repo remove`, `repo sync` orphan cleanup, or manual deletion — these symlinks break silently.

The current user experience is poor:

1. **`aimgr list` silently hides broken resources** — `List()` in `pkg/install/installer.go` has `continue` statements (lines 454, 493, 542) that skip broken symlinks. Resources just vanish from output with no indication.
2. **AI tools silently lose access** — Claude Code, OpenCode, etc. lose skills/commands with no error. The user's AI setup degrades invisibly.
3. **`aimgr verify --fix` doesn't actually fix** — It removes broken symlinks but has TODO comments (lines 466, 474) for reinstallation that were never implemented. The `manager` parameter is `interface{}` and can't call install functions.
4. **No proactive detection** — Users must remember to run `aimgr verify`. There's no check at `aimgr list` time.
5. **Manifest desync** — `ai.package.yaml` still lists the resource as expected, but `List()` doesn't return it, creating a confusing gap.

This epic is the 4th in the stability series (after Source Identity, Reliable Add/Remove, and Sync Lifecycle) and focuses on the PROJECT side of the equation — making installed resources resilient to repo changes.

## Goals

- `aimgr list` shows broken resources with a clear `✗` indicator instead of hiding them
- A health summary is shown when broken resources are detected (e.g., "⚠ 2 broken resources found. Run 'aimgr verify --fix' to repair.")
- `aimgr verify --fix` actually reinstalls resources that still exist in the repo
- Clear messaging when resources are gone from the repo (unrecoverable) vs temporarily missing
- Comprehensive test coverage for broken-symlink scenarios across list, verify, and install

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child tasks completed
- [ ] Broken resources visible in `aimgr list` output (not silently hidden)
- [ ] Health warning shown when broken resources detected
- [ ] `aimgr verify --fix` reinstalls recoverable resources
- [ ] Unrecoverable resources get clear messaging
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All 4 tasks and acceptance gate closed. Broken symlink detection and recovery fully implemented: List() returns broken resources with HealthBroken status, aimgr list shows broken resources with ✗ status column and stderr warning, aimgr verify --fix actually reinstalls recoverable resources and shows clear messaging for unrecoverable ones, 16 new tests covering all scenarios. Verified via E2E testing by gate agent.

title: Fix repo add ordering: manifest tracking happens after import
description:
## Description

Task ai-config-manager-8pow assumes that `addSourceToManifest()` runs BEFORE `addBulkFromLocalWithMode()`, so the source ID can be retrieved from the manifest and passed to the import pipeline.

**However, the actual code in `cmd/repo_add.go` (lines 110-135) does the opposite:**

1. First: `addBulkFromLocalWithMode()` or `addBulkFromGitHub()` runs the import
2. Then: `addSourceToManifest()` creates the manifest entry

This means at import time, the manifest source (and its auto-generated ID) does not yet exist. The task agent implementing 8pow will discover this discrepancy and may get confused or implement it incorrectly.

## Resolution Needed

Task ai-config-manager-8pow's instructions need to account for the actual ordering. Two approaches:

**Option A (Recommended)**: Reverse the order — call `addSourceToManifest()` first (before import), then pass the generated source ID to the import pipeline. This is a cleaner flow.

**Option B**: Generate the source ID independently (using `GenerateSourceID()` directly on a temporary Source struct) before the import, then pass it through. The manifest entry gets created after import as before.

The planner should add a clarification comment to task 8pow specifying which approach to use, OR the task agent can choose Option A since it's cleaner.

## Impact

Without this fix, the task agent will hit a wall when trying to "retrieve the source from manifest to get its ID" — because the manifest entry doesn't exist at that point in the flow.
close_reason: Resolved via comment on task 8pow: Use Option B (generate ID independently before import). No code ordering change needed.

title: Remove orphaned resources after sync and show summary
description:
## Description

After detecting which resources were removed from their sources (previous task), actually remove them from the repo and show a clear summary.

This replaces the TODO at `cmd/repo_sync.go:259-264`.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Remove detected orphans

In `runSync()`, after the source processing loop (where the TODO currently is):

```go
// Remove resources that were deleted from their sources
if !syncDryRunFlag && len(removedResources) > 0 {
    fmt.Printf("\nRemoving %d resource(s) no longer in sources:\n", len(removedResources))
    
    removeCount := 0
    for _, res := range removedResources {
        fmt.Printf("  - %s/%s\n", res.Type, res.Name)
        if err := manager.Remove(res.Name, res.Type); err != nil {
            fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  ⚠ Warning: failed to remove %s/%s: %v\n",
                res.Type, res.Name, err)
        } else {
            removeCount++
        }
    }
    fmt.Printf("✓ Removed %d resource(s)\n", removeCount)
} else if syncDryRunFlag && len(removedResources) > 0 {
    fmt.Printf("\nWould remove %d resource(s) no longer in sources:\n", len(removedResources))
    for _, res := range removedResources {
        fmt.Printf("  - %s/%s\n", res.Type, res.Name)
    }
}
```

### Step 2: Warn about breaking project symlinks

Before removing, check if any of the resources are installed in projects:

```go
// Warn about project symlinks that will break
// (Reuse logic from Epic 2 task .4 if available, otherwise simple warning)
if len(removedResources) > 0 {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "\n⚠ Removed resources may have active project installations.\n")
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Run 'aimgr project verify' to check for broken symlinks.\n\n")
}
```

### Step 3: Enhanced summary

Update the sync summary at the end to include removal counts:

```go
fmt.Printf("Sync Complete: %d/%d sources synced, %d resource(s) removed\n",
    sourcesProcessed, len(manifest.Sources), removeCount)
```

### Step 4: Handle dry-run mode

In dry-run mode, don't remove but show what would be removed (already handled in Step 1).

## Files to Modify

- cmd/repo_sync.go — Replace TODO with removal logic, update summary

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Resources deleted from sources are removed from repo during sync
- [ ] Removal only happens for successfully synced sources
- [ ] Dry-run shows what would be removed without removing
- [ ] Warning about project symlinks shown before removal
- [ ] Summary includes removal count
- [ ] Resources with broken metadata (no source) are not touched
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] go vet passes

(4) Added dry-run support (shows what would be removed without removing). (5) Updated summary to include removal count. (6) Fixed Manager.Remove() to use os.Lstat instead of os.Stat for dangling symlink support. (7) Added 4 new tests: dry-run doesn't remove, git commit verification, removal-only-for-successful-sources, and updated 2 existing tests to verify actual removal.

title: Sync Full Lifecycle — Orphan Cleanup During Sync
description:
## Description

The `repo sync` command currently only ADDS and UPDATES resources. It never REMOVES resources that were deleted from a source. There is an explicit TODO at `cmd/repo_sync.go:259-264`:

```go
// TODO: Orphan cleanup
// After syncing all sources, find and remove resources that:
// 1. Have metadata.SourceName set
// 2. That source name is no longer in manifest.Sources
// This ensures resources from removed sources are cleaned up
```

However, the actual need is broader. Sync should handle three cases:
1. **New resources** in source → add to repo (already works)
2. **Changed resources** in source → update in repo (already works via force mode)
3. **Deleted resources** from source → remove from repo (NOT implemented)

The design decision: **no --delete flag**. Sync always handles all three cases. Sources are the unit of management — you add a source, you get all its resources. When a resource disappears from the source, it should disappear from the repo on next sync.

## Safety Constraints

- Only remove resources from sources that synced SUCCESSFULLY. If a source fails to sync (network error, path not found), do NOT remove its resources.
- Warn about breaking project symlinks before removing.
- Provide a summary of what was removed.

## Goals

- Sync detects and removes resources that no longer exist in their source
- Failed sources don't trigger orphan removal for their resources
- Clear summary of added/updated/removed resources per source
- Project symlink warnings for removed resources

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child tasks completed
- [ ] Resources deleted from source are removed on next sync
- [ ] Failed sources don't have their resources removed
- [ ] Summary shows added/updated/removed counts
- [ ] Warning shown for breaking project symlinks
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All 8 child tasks and acceptance gate closed. Sync now handles the full lifecycle: add, update, and remove resources. Orphan cleanup detects resources deleted from sources and removes them on next sync, with safety for failed sources, dry-run support, cross-source collision handling, project symlink warnings, and comprehensive test coverage (unit + E2E).

title: Reliable Source Add & Remove
description:
## Description

The `repo add` and `repo remove` commands must work 100% reliably as symmetric operations. Currently there are gaps:

1. **No duplicate source ID detection**: Two different sources could theoretically produce the same hash (collision). More practically, adding the same source twice by different paths (e.g., relative vs absolute, or symlinked) should be detected.
2. **Incomplete cleanup on remove**: `repo remove` removes the manifest entry and resource files/metadata, but does NOT clean up the source metadata entry in `.metadata/sources.json`. This leaves stale entries.
3. **HasSource uses name-only matching**: The orphan detection in `repo remove` matches by `source_name` only. After Epic 1 adds source IDs, it should match by `source_id` first (already partially addressed in Epic 1 task .2, but needs to be wired into the remove flow).
4. **No installed project cascade warning**: When removing a source's resources from the repo, installed project symlinks pointing to those resources become broken. No warning is shown.

## Goals

- `repo add` detects duplicate sources by source ID (not just name/path/URL string matching)
- `repo remove` cleans up ALL artifacts: manifest entry, resources, resource metadata, source metadata, and package metadata for source-only packages
- `repo remove` warns about installed project symlinks that will break
- The add/remove cycle is fully symmetric: add then remove leaves the repo in the same state as before

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child tasks completed
- [ ] Duplicate source detection by ID works
- [ ] repo remove cleans up source metadata entry
- [ ] repo remove warns about breaking project symlinks
- [ ] Add then remove leaves no artifacts behind
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All 7 child tasks closed including acceptance gate or7i.6. Epic goals achieved: duplicate source detection by ID works, repo remove cleans up all artifacts (manifest, resources, metadata, source metadata, package metadata), repo remove warns about breaking project symlinks, add/remove cycle is fully symmetric.

title: [GATE] Epic Acceptance: Stable Source Identity
description:
## Gate Criteria

This task serves as the acceptance gate for Epic 1 (ai-config-manager-qvzz).
It should be verified by beads-verify-agent after all other Epic 1 tasks are closed.

### All Must Pass

- All Epic 1 tasks closed (.1 through .6)
- Source IDs are deterministic: same location always produces same ID
- URL normalization handles .git suffix, casing, trailing slashes
- Renaming a source name in manifest preserves resource connections
- Existing repos migrate transparently on first manifest load
- repo add stores source_id in all resource metadata
- repo sync stores source_id and correct source_name in all resource metadata
- All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- New integration test for rename scenario passes
- go vet and make test pass clean

## Owner
beads-verify-agent

## Verification Steps

1. Run make test — all tests pass
2. Check a resource metadata file after repo add — has source_id field
3. Check a resource metadata file after repo sync — has source_id field
4. Rename a source in manifest, sync, verify resources not orphaned
5. Run repo verify — no orphan warnings related to source identity
close_reason: All 8 criteria verified: (1) All epic tasks closed (.1-.4, 8pow, nt02, 9peg, 2d5o). (2) Source IDs deterministic — SHA256 hash with src- prefix + 12 hex chars, URL normalization handles .git/casing/slashes. (3) Rename test covers name rename preserving connections via source_id and path change creating new ID. (4) Migration auto-generates IDs on Load() and persists to disk. (5) repo add computes source_id via temp Source struct for both local and GitHub. (6) repo sync passes src.Name and src.ID correctly. (7) make test — all tests pass clean. (8) go vet — clean, no issues.

title: Thread source ID through repo sync and fix source name passthrough bug
description:
## Description

This task fixes TWO problems in `cmd/repo_sync.go`:

1. **Bug fix**: `syncSource()` at line 142 calls `addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath, manager, "", mode)` — it passes empty string for source name instead of `src.Name`. This causes resource metadata to get `source_name` derived from `filepath.Base()` instead of the manifest name.

2. **Source ID threading**: Pass `src.ID` (added in task .2) through to the import pipeline so synced resources get proper `source_id` in their metadata.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Fix syncSource() in cmd/repo_sync.go (~line 73)

The syncSource function receives a `*repomanifest.Source`. Update the call to `addBulkFromLocalWithMode`:

Before (BROKEN):
```go
addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath, manager, "", mode)
```

After (FIXED):
```go
addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath, manager, src.Name, src.ID, mode)
```

This fixes both the name bug AND threads the source ID.

### Step 2: For GitHub sources in sync

If syncSource handles GitHub URLs differently (check the code path), ensure the same fix applies — pass `src.Name` and `src.ID` through `addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter`.

### Step 3: Verify source metadata timestamps use source ID

In `cmd/repo_sync.go`, check where source metadata (pkg/sourcemetadata) is updated after sync. If it currently uses source name as key, update to use source ID when available (with name fallback for migration).

Look for calls to `sourcemetadata.UpdateLastSync()` or similar, and pass source ID.

### Step 4: Write a unit test for the fix

Create or update test in `cmd/repo_sync_test.go` or `test/e2e/sync_test.go`:

1. Create a temp source directory with a resource
2. Add to manifest with a specific name (e.g., "my-tools") and path that has a DIFFERENT basename (e.g., "/tmp/tools-v2")
3. Run sync
4. Verify resource metadata has `source_name: "my-tools"` (not "tools-v2")
5. Verify resource metadata has `source_id: "src-<hash>"` matching the manifest

## Files to Modify

- cmd/repo_sync.go — Fix syncSource() call, thread source name and ID
- test/e2e/sync_test.go or cmd/repo_sync_test.go — Add test for name/ID passthrough

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] syncSource passes src.Name to addBulkFromLocalWithMode (bug fix)
- [ ] syncSource passes src.ID to addBulkFromLocalWithMode (ID threading)
- [ ] After sync, resource metadata has correct source_name from manifest (not filepath.Base)
- [ ] After sync, resource metadata has correct source_id
- [ ] Source metadata timestamps use source ID as key when available
- [ ] Test verifies name mismatch scenario (manifest name ≠ directory name)
- [ ] All existing sync tests pass
- [ ] go vet passes

## Notes

- This is the critical bug fix task. The existing bug means any source where the manifest name differs from the directory basename gets wrong metadata.
- Depends on task .2 (source ID field on Source struct) and task .3 (updated function signatures).
close_reason:
Fixed both bugs in syncSource():
1. BUG FIX: syncSource now passes src.Name (not empty string) to addBulkFromLocalWithMode, so resource metadata gets correct source_name from manifest instead of filepath.Base().
2. SOURCE ID THREADING: syncSource now passes src.ID through to addBulkFromLocalWithMode, so synced resources get proper source_id in metadata.
3. SOURCE METADATA: Updated sync's SourceState updates to also set SourceID when available.
4. FUNCTION SIGNATURE: Added sourceName parameter to addBulkFromLocalWithMode (6 params total). When non-empty, it takes precedence over nameFlag/filepath.Base.
5. TEST: Added TestSyncSourceNamePassthrough integration test that verifies manifest name 'my-tools' is used instead of directory basename 'tools-v2', and source_id matches GenerateSourceID output.
All existing sync tests pass. go vet passes.

title: Thread source ID through repo add pipeline
description:
## Description

When a user runs `repo add`, the source ID must be computed and threaded all the way through the import pipeline so every resource's metadata includes `source_id`.

Currently `addSourceToManifest()` creates a Source and adds it to the manifest. After task .2, `AddSource()` auto-generates the ID. This task ensures that ID flows from the manifest through `addBulkFromLocalWithMode()` and `addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter()` into `BulkImportOptions.SourceID`, so `importResource()` writes it to each resource's metadata.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Update addBulkFromLocalWithMode (cmd/repo_add.go ~line 612)

This function currently takes `(sourcePath string, manager *repo.Manager, sourceName string, mode string)`.

1. Add a `sourceID string` parameter:
   ```go
   func addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath string, manager *repo.Manager, sourceName string, sourceID string, mode string) error
   ```

2. Set `sourceID` on the BulkImportOptions struct before calling `manager.AddBulk()`:
   ```go
   opts := repo.BulkImportOptions{
       SourceName: sourceName,
       SourceID:   sourceID,
       // ... existing fields
   }
   ```

### Step 2: Update addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter (cmd/repo_add.go)

Same pattern — add `sourceID string` parameter, pass it into BulkImportOptions.

### Step 3: Update importFromLocalPathWithMode (cmd/repo_add.go ~line 387)

For single-resource imports that aren't from a named source, sourceID can remain empty. No change needed here — just verify it passes empty string through.

### Step 4: Update all call sites

Search for every call to `addBulkFromLocalWithMode` and `addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter`:

1. In the `repo add` command handler (local path flow):
   - After `addSourceToManifest()` returns, retrieve the source from manifest to get its ID
   - Pass the ID to `addBulkFromLocalWithMode()`

2. In the `repo add` command handler (GitHub URL flow):
   - Same pattern: get source from manifest, pass ID

3. In `syncSource()` (cmd/repo_sync.go ~line 142) — this is currently broken:
   - Currently calls: `addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath, manager, "", mode)`
   - This will be fixed properly in task .4, but for now update the signature to compile:
   ```go
   addBulkFromLocalWithMode(sourcePath, manager, "", "", mode)
   ```

### Step 5: Verify single-resource add still works

For `repo add <path-to-single-resource>` (no source), the pipeline should work with empty SourceID. Verify no crash or unexpected behavior.

## Files to Modify

- cmd/repo_add.go — Update function signatures and call sites
- cmd/repo_sync.go — Update call to addBulkFromLocalWithMode to match new signature (placeholder empty ID, fixed properly in .4)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] addBulkFromLocalWithMode accepts sourceID parameter
- [ ] addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter accepts sourceID parameter
- [ ] repo add (local source) stores source_id in every resource's metadata
- [ ] repo add (GitHub source) stores source_id in every resource's metadata
- [ ] repo add (single resource, no source) works with empty source_id
- [ ] syncSource call site compiles (placeholder fix, full fix in .4)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] go vet passes

## Notes

- This task focuses on `repo add`. The `repo sync` pipeline gets its proper fix in task .4.
- The key insight: source ID is generated in AddSource() (task .2), so by the time we call the bulk import, the manifest already has the ID. We just need to read it back.
close_reason: Threaded source ID through the repo add pipeline. Changes: (1) addBulkFromLocalWithMode now accepts sourceID parameter, (2) addBulkFromGitHubWithFilter now accepts sourceID parameter, (3) repo add local flow computes source ID from filepath.Abs + GenerateSourceID before import, (4) repo add GitHub flow computes source ID from URL + GenerateSourceID before import, (5) importFromLocalPathWithMode passes sourceID into BulkImportOptions, (6) syncSource call site passes empty string for sourceID placeholder, (7) addBulkFromLocalWithFilter passes empty string for backward compat, (8) all existing tests pass, go vet passes.

title: Add source ID field to manifest, resource metadata, and source metadata
description:
## Description

Add the source_id field to all three data structures that track source identity: the manifest (ai.repo.yaml), resource metadata (per-resource JSON), and source metadata (sources.json). Keep existing source_name fields for backward compatibility.

## Instructions

### Step 1: Add ID field to Source struct in manifest

In pkg/repomanifest/manifest.go:

1. Add field to Source struct:
   ```go
   ID string `yaml:"id,omitempty"`
   ```
   Place it as the first field (before Name) since it's the primary identifier.

2. Update AddSource() to auto-generate ID if not set:
   ```go
   if source.ID == "" {
       source.ID = GenerateSourceID(source)
   }
   ```

3. Update Validate() to check for duplicate IDs across sources (in addition to existing duplicate name check).

4. Update GetSource() to also match by ID. Current matching: name, path, URL. Add ID matching with highest priority.

### Step 2: Add source_id field to ResourceMetadata

In pkg/metadata/metadata.go:

1. Add to ResourceMetadata struct:
   ```go
   SourceID string `json:"source_id,omitempty"`
   ```

2. Add to PackageMetadata struct:
   ```go
   SourceID string `json:"source_id,omitempty"`
   ```

3. Update HasSource() function to check SourceID first, fall back to SourceName:
   ```go
   func HasSource(name string, resourceType ResourceType, sourceIdentifier, repoPath string) bool {
       metadata, err := Load(name, resourceType, repoPath)
       if err != nil { return false }
       // Check by ID first
       if metadata.SourceID != "" && metadata.SourceID == sourceIdentifier {
           return true
       }
       // Fall back to name for backward compat
       return metadata.SourceName != "" && metadata.SourceName == sourceIdentifier
   }
   ```

### Step 3: Update source metadata to support ID keys

In pkg/sourcemetadata/metadata.go:

The Sources map is currently keyed by source name. It should support being keyed by source ID. Since this is an internal file, we can:

1. Keep the map as `map[string]*SourceState` — the key can be either name or ID
2. Add a helper method to look up by either:
   ```go
   func (m *SourceMetadata) GetByNameOrID(key string) *SourceState
   ```
3. When writing new entries, use source ID as key if available

### Step 4: Thread source_id through BulkImportOptions

In pkg/repo/bulk.go:

1. Add SourceID field to BulkImportOptions:
   ```go
   SourceID string // Source ID from manifest (hash-based)
   ```

2. Add SourceID field to ImportOptions:
   ```go
   SourceID string
   ```

3. In importResource(), when creating/updating metadata, set SourceID from opts.SourceID

### Step 5: Update Manager.HasSource() in pkg/repo/remove.go

1. Update HasSource() to check metadata.SourceID first, fall back to SourceName
2. Update the debug logging to show both source_id and source_name

## Files to Modify

- pkg/repomanifest/manifest.go — Add ID to Source, update AddSource/Validate/GetSource
- pkg/metadata/metadata.go — Add source_id to ResourceMetadata and PackageMetadata, update HasSource
- pkg/sourcemetadata/metadata.go — Support ID-keyed entries
- pkg/repo/bulk.go — Add SourceID to BulkImportOptions and ImportOptions, thread through
- pkg/repo/remove.go — Update HasSource to check SourceID first

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Source struct has ID field (yaml:"id,omitempty")
- [ ] AddSource generates ID automatically if not set
- [ ] Validate rejects duplicate source IDs
- [ ] GetSource matches by ID in addition to name/path/URL
- [ ] ResourceMetadata has SourceID field (json:"source_id,omitempty")
- [ ] PackageMetadata has SourceID field (json:"source_id,omitempty")
- [ ] HasSource checks SourceID first, falls back to SourceName
- [ ] BulkImportOptions and ImportOptions have SourceID field
- [ ] importResource sets SourceID on metadata during import
- [ ] Manager.HasSource checks SourceID first with debug logging
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no breaking changes)
- [ ] JSON/YAML serialization works correctly with new fields (omitempty for backward compat)

## Notes

- All new fields use omitempty so existing data without IDs still parses correctly
- This task adds the fields and wiring but does NOT change how repo add/sync call the pipeline — that's in the next tasks
- The HasSource fallback to SourceName ensures backward compat during migration
close_reason:
Implemented source_id field across all three data structures:

1. **manifest.go (Source struct)**: Added ID field (yaml:"id,omitempty"), auto-generation in AddSource(), duplicate ID validation in Validate(), and ID-first matching in GetSource().

2. **metadata.go (ResourceMetadata & PackageMetadata)**: Added SourceID field (json:"source_id,omitempty") to both structs. Updated HasSource() to check SourceID first with SourceName fallback.

3. **sourcemetadata (SourceState)**: Added SourceID field (json:"source_id,omitempty"). Map key remains source name per review comment.

4. **bulk.go (BulkImportOptions & ImportOptions)**: Added SourceID field, threaded through to importResource() and importPackage().

5. **add.go**: Set SourceID from opts.SourceID when creating ResourceMetadata and PackageMetadata.

6. **remove.go (Manager.HasSource)**: Checks SourceID first, falls back to SourceName. Debug logging shows both source_id and source_name with match_by_id/match_by_name details.

All existing tests pass (repomanifest, metadata, sourcemetadata, repo packages). go vet clean.

title: Add GenerateSourceID function and tests
description:
## Description

Create the core ID generation function that produces a deterministic hash-based source ID from a source's canonical location (URL or absolute path).

## Instructions

### Step 1: Create pkg/repomanifest/sourceid.go

Add function GenerateSourceID(source *Source) string:

1. If source has URL:
   - Normalize: lowercase, strip trailing slash, strip .git suffix
   - Compute SHA256 of normalized URL string
   - Return "src-" + first 12 hex chars of hash
2. If source has Path:
   - Resolve to absolute path using filepath.Abs()
   - Compute SHA256 of absolute path string
   - Return "src-" + first 12 hex chars of hash
3. If neither: return empty string (invalid source)

### Step 2: Create pkg/repomanifest/sourceid_test.go

Test cases:
- Same URL with/without .git suffix → same ID
- Same URL with different casing → same ID
- Same URL with/without trailing slash → same ID
- Different URLs → different IDs
- Local absolute path → stable ID
- Same relative path resolved to same absolute → same ID
- Empty source → empty string
- Source with both URL and Path (shouldn't happen, but test that URL takes precedence)

## Files to Modify

- pkg/repomanifest/sourceid.go (create)
- pkg/repomanifest/sourceid_test.go (create)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GenerateSourceID returns stable deterministic IDs
- [ ] URL normalization handles .git suffix, casing, trailing slashes
- [ ] Path resolution uses filepath.Abs
- [ ] ID format is "src-" + 12 hex chars (16 chars total)
- [ ] All test cases pass
- [ ] go vet passes on new files
close_reason: Implemented GenerateSourceID function in pkg/repomanifest/sourceid.go and comprehensive tests in sourceid_test.go. All acceptance criteria met: deterministic IDs, URL normalization (.git suffix, casing, trailing slashes), path resolution via filepath.Abs, ID format 'src-' + 12 hex chars, all 13 test cases pass, go vet clean.

title: Stable Source Identity — Hash-Based Source IDs
description:
## Description

Source identity is currently based on the user-facing `name` field in ai.repo.yaml. This name is stored in every resource's metadata as `source_name`. Renaming a source in the manifest severs all connections to its resources, making orphan detection fail silently.

This epic introduces a deterministic hash-based source ID that is immutable for a given source location (URL or path). The name becomes a freely renameable label.

## Goals

- Every source gets a stable ID derived from its canonical location (URL or absolute path)
- Source ID is stored in the manifest (ai.repo.yaml), resource metadata, and source metadata
- The existing source_name field is kept for backward compatibility and human readability
- Renaming a source in ai.repo.yaml does NOT break resource-to-source connections
- Existing repos migrate transparently — no user action required

## Design Decision

ID format: `src-` prefix + first 12 hex chars of SHA256 hash
- GitHub/Git: SHA256(lowercase(trimSuffix(url, ".git")))[:12]
- Local path: SHA256(resolvedAbsolutePath)[:12]

Example: `src-a3f8e9b2c1d0`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All child tasks completed
- [ ] Source IDs are deterministic and stable
- [ ] Renaming a source name preserves resource connections
- [ ] Existing repos get IDs on first use (migration)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed
close_reason: All 7 tasks and acceptance gate closed. Stable hash-based source IDs implemented: GenerateSourceID function, ID fields on all metadata structs, threaded through add/sync pipelines, auto-migration on manifest load, integration tests prove rename preserves connections. Gate verified by beads-verify-agent — all tests pass, go vet clean.

title: Epic Acceptance: Robust Source Tracking & Sync Orphan Cleanup
description:
## Gate Criteria

This gate verifies the full epic delivers on its promises. All criteria must be met.

### Functional Requirements

- [ ] Source IDs are generated deterministically from URL/path
- [ ] Source names can be renamed in ai.repo.yaml without breaking resource connections
- [ ] repo sync removes resources that were deleted from their source
- [ ] repo sync does NOT remove resources when their source failed to sync
- [ ] repo remove correctly finds orphans using source ID (not just name)
- [ ] repo verify detects source tracking inconsistencies
- [ ] repo verify --fix cleans up broken package references

### Migration and Backward Compat

- [ ] Existing repos without source IDs get IDs on first manifest load
- [ ] Resources with source_name but no source_id still work (HasSource fallback)
- [ ] No breaking changes to CLI interface
- [ ] No user action required to migrate

### Testing

- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] New unit tests for source ID generation
- [ ] Integration test: add source, delete resource in source, sync, verify orphan removed
- [ ] Integration test: rename source in manifest, sync, verify resources still tracked
- [ ] Integration test: repo verify detects unmigrated resources

### End-to-End Scenario

Run this full scenario:
1. Create temp source with 3 resources (command, skill, agent)
2. aimgr repo add source: verify 3 resources imported with source_id in metadata
3. Rename source in ai.repo.yaml (change name field only), repo sync, verify all 3 resources still tracked
4. Delete one resource from source, repo sync, verify orphan removed, 2 remain
5. repo verify: no errors
6. aimgr repo remove source: verify remaining resources cleaned up

## Owner

beads-verify-agent
