title: Migrate all table usages to responsive column sizing
description:
## Description
Update all table rendering locations identified in the audit (ai-config-manager-upw) to use the new responsive column sizing features.

## CRITICAL: Truncation Removal Required
**All tables have hardcoded truncateString() calls that MUST be removed during migration.** See NOTES section for complete list and step-by-step instructions. Failure to remove these will cause:
- Double-truncation (text truncated twice)
- Responsive sizing ignored (40/60 char limits prevent dynamic growth)
- Inconsistent behavior across terminal widths

## Instructions
For each table usage identified in audit:

1. **Add responsive configuration:**
   ```go
   table := output.NewTable(headers...)
   table.WithResponsive().
         WithDynamicColumn(lastColumnIndex)
   ```

2. **Remove hardcoded truncateString() calls** (see NOTES for complete list):
   - cmd/list_installed.go: 3 calls (lines 325, 339, 353)
   - cmd/list.go: 5 calls (lines 231, 242, 253, 264, 284)
   - cmd/repo_import.go: 1 call (line 703)
   - Pass full description text to table.AddRow() instead

3. **Configure dynamic columns:**
   - cmd/list.go (repo list): Dynamic column = Description (index 1)
   - cmd/list_installed.go: Dynamic column = Description (index 3)
   - cmd/repo_import.go: Dynamic column = Description (index 2)
   - cmd/repo_verify.go: Each table needs appropriate dynamic column

4. **Test each migrated table manually:**
   - Wide terminal (120+ cols): Descriptions show MORE than 40/60 chars
   - Narrow terminal (80 cols): Dynamic truncation with "..."
   - Very narrow terminal (60 cols): Graceful column hiding
   - Verify NO double-truncation occurs

## Example Migration
```go
// BEFORE
desc := truncateString(cmd.Description, 40)  // ← Remove this
table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, desc)

// AFTER
table := output.NewTable("Name", "Targets", "Sync", "Description")
table.WithResponsive().WithDynamicColumn(3)  // ← Add this
// ...
table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, cmd.Description)  // ← Full text
```

## Files to Modify
- cmd/list.go (5 truncation calls)
- cmd/list_installed.go (3 truncation calls)
- cmd/repo_import.go (1 truncation call)
- cmd/repo_verify.go (responsive config only)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All table usages identified in audit are migrated
- [ ] Each table has appropriate dynamic column configured
- [ ] **All 9 truncateString() calls removed from Description columns**
- [ ] **Full descriptions passed to table.AddRow()**
- [ ] Manual testing shows correct responsive behavior:
  - Wide terminals show MORE text than 40/60 char limits
  - Narrow terminals truncate dynamically
  - NO double-truncation occurs
- [ ] No visual regressions in table output
- [ ] Tables use full terminal width
- [ ] Text truncates with "..." when needed
- [ ] Columns hide gracefully in narrow terminals

## Dependencies
- Depends on: ai-config-manager-upw (audit)
- Depends on: ai-config-manager-6ju (implementation)
- Depends on: ai-config-manager-qjn (truncation removal documentation)

## Notes
See NOTES section for complete list of all 9 truncateString() calls with line numbers, step-by-step migration pattern, and testing requirements.
notes:
## CRITICAL STEP: Remove Hardcoded Truncation

**WARNING**: All tables have hardcoded `truncateString()` calls that MUST be removed during migration to avoid double-truncation and enable responsive sizing.

### Complete List of Truncation Calls to Remove

#### cmd/list_installed.go (3 calls)
```go
// Line 325 - Commands
desc := truncateString(cmd.Description, 40)  // ← REMOVE

// Line 339 - Skills  
desc := truncateString(skill.Description, 40)  // ← REMOVE

// Line 353 - Agents
desc := truncateString(agent.Description, 40)  // ← REMOVE
```

#### cmd/list.go (4 calls in outputWithPackagesTable)
```go
// Line 231 - Commands
desc := truncateString(cmd.Description, 60)  // ← REMOVE

// Line 242 - Skills
desc := truncateString(skill.Description, 60)  // ← REMOVE

// Line 253 - Agents  
desc := truncateString(agent.Description, 60)  // ← REMOVE

// Line 264 - Packages
desc := truncateString(pkg.Description, 60)  // ← REMOVE
```

#### cmd/list.go (1 call in outputPackagesTable)
```go
// Line 284 - Packages
desc := truncateString(pkg.Description, 60)  // ← REMOVE
```

#### cmd/repo_import.go (1 call in selectResource)
```go
// Line 703 - Interactive selector
desc = truncateString(desc, 60)  // ← REMOVE
```

### Migration Pattern

**BEFORE (current code):**
```go
desc := truncateString(cmd.Description, 40)
table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, desc)
```

**AFTER (with responsive sizing):**
```go
// Pass full description - responsive sizing handles truncation
table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, cmd.Description)
```

### Step-by-Step Migration Instructions

For EACH table location:

1. **Add responsive configuration:**
   ```go
   table := output.NewTable(headers...)
   table.WithResponsive().
         WithDynamicColumn(lastColumnIndex)
   ```

2. **Remove truncateString() calls:**
   - Delete the `desc := truncateString(...)` line
   - Pass the full description field directly to `table.AddRow()`

3. **Example for cmd/list_installed.go (commands section):**
   ```go
   // BEFORE
   for _, cmd := range commands {
       desc := truncateString(cmd.Description, 40)  // ← Delete this line
       targets := strings.Join(cmd.Targets, ", ")
       resourceRef := fmt.Sprintf("command/%s", cmd.Name)
       syncSymbol := formatSyncStatus(projectPath, resourceRef, len(cmd.Targets) > 0)
       table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, desc)  // ← Change desc to cmd.Description
   }
   
   // AFTER
   for _, cmd := range commands {
       targets := strings.Join(cmd.Targets, ", ")
       resourceRef := fmt.Sprintf("command/%s", cmd.Name)
       syncSymbol := formatSyncStatus(projectPath, resourceRef, len(cmd.Targets) > 0)
       table.AddRow(resourceRef, targets, syncSymbol, cmd.Description)  // ← Full description
   }
   ```

4. **Repeat for skills and agents sections** in same file

5. **Repeat pattern for each file** (list.go, repo_import.go)

### Testing Requirements

After removing truncation, verify:

1. **Wide terminal (120+ columns):**
   - Descriptions show MORE than 40/60 characters
   - No artificial truncation at 40 or 60 chars
   - Text uses available space

2. **Narrow terminal (80 columns):**
   - Descriptions truncate dynamically with "..."
   - Truncation point varies based on other column widths
   - No double-truncation (no "..." in middle of text)

3. **Very narrow terminal (60 columns):**
   - Last column still visible
   - Graceful truncation

### Why This Matters

**Double-truncation problem:**
```
Original: "This is a very long description that needs to be shown"
After hardcoded truncate(40): "This is a very long description that n..."
After responsive truncate(80): "This is a very long description that n..." (wasted space!)
```

**Correct behavior (no hardcoded truncation):**
```
Original: "This is a very long description that needs to be shown"
Terminal width 120, available 80: "This is a very long description that needs to be shown"
Terminal width 80, available 40: "This is a very long description that..."
```

### Acceptance Criteria Additions

- [ ] All 9 truncateString() calls removed from Description columns
- [ ] Full descriptions passed to table.AddRow() 
- [ ] Manual testing confirms NO 40-char or 60-char limits remain
- [ ] Wide terminals show significantly more text than before
- [ ] Truncation is dynamic based on terminal width, not hardcoded

### Total Changes Required

- **3 files** to modify
- **9 truncateString() calls** to remove
- **4 functions** to update:
  - cmd/list_installed.go: outputInstalledTable()
  - cmd/list.go: outputWithPackagesTable()
  - cmd/list.go: outputPackagesTable()  
  - cmd/repo_import.go: selectResource()

See task ai-config-manager-qjn for complete context and rationale.
close_reason: Successfully migrated all 7 table rendering locations to use responsive column sizing. Removed all 9 hardcoded truncateString() calls. Each table now has WithResponsive(), WithDynamicColumn(), and WithMinColumnWidths() configured. Tables automatically adapt to terminal width with proper truncation and minimum widths.

title: Implement responsive column sizing in pkg/output/table.go
description:
## Description
Enhance the table rendering system to support responsive column sizing with dynamic width allocation, text truncation, and graceful column hiding.

## Column Hiding Specification (from ai-config-manager-4fc)

### Priority Order
Columns hide **right-to-left** (rightmost column hides first). This preserves the most critical columns (Name, Type) while progressively hiding less essential columns (Description, Sync, Targets).

### Minimum Column Widths
| Column Type | Min Width | 
|-------------|-----------|
| Name/ID | 10 chars |
| Type | 8 chars |
| Status | 6 chars |
| Targets | 12 chars |
| Sync | 5 chars |
| Description | 15 chars |
| Generic Text | 10 chars |

### Dynamic Column Behavior
- The **rightmost visible column** is always dynamic (fills remaining space)
- Dynamic column minimum width: **15 chars**
- When a column hides, the new rightmost column becomes dynamic
- Text truncates with `...` ellipsis using `WrapTruncate` mode

### Width Thresholds
| Terminal Width | Behavior | Example (4 columns) |
|----------------|----------|---------------------|
| ≥80 chars | All columns visible | Name, Targets, Sync, Description |
| 60-79 chars | Hide rightmost | Name, Targets, Sync |
| 40-59 chars | Hide 2 rightmost | Name, Targets |
| 25-39 chars | Hide 3 rightmost | Name only |
| <15 chars | Disable responsive mode | Fallback to non-responsive |

### Edge Cases
- **Single column mode** (<25 chars): Show Name only, allocate full width
- **Below minimum** (<15 chars): Disable responsive mode entirely
- **Long content**: Truncate with ellipsis, preserve ≥10 visible chars

## Implementation Instructions

1. **Update TableOptions** (`pkg/output/types.go`):
   - Add `DynamicColumn int` (index of stretch column, -1 = rightmost)
   - Add `MinColumnWidths []int` (minimum width per column)
   - Add `MinTerminalWidth int` (default: 15)

2. **Add TableBuilder methods** (`pkg/output/table.go`):
   - `WithDynamicColumn(index int)` - mark stretch column
   - `WithMinColumnWidths(widths ...int)` - set minimums

3. **Implement column visibility logic**:
   - Create `determineVisibleColumns(termWidth, headers, minWidths)`
   - Calculate border overhead: `(numCols * 3) + 1`
   - Iterate left-to-right, hide columns that don't fit

4. **Implement width allocation**:
   - Create `allocateColumnWidths(termWidth, visibleCols, minWidths)`
   - Allocate minimum widths to fixed columns (all except last)
   - Give remaining space to dynamic column (last visible)
   - Enforce dynamic minimum (15 chars)

5. **Update renderTable()**:
   - Detect terminal width using existing `NewTerminal()`
   - Determine visible columns
   - Allocate widths
   - Filter headers/rows to visible columns only
   - Apply widths using `WithColumnWidths(map[int]int)`
   - Set `WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate)` for ellipsis

6. **Use tablewriter v1.1.3 API** (see ai-config-manager-pjy notes):
   - Constructor: `tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout, options...)`
   - Headers: `table.Header(headers...)`
   - Options: `WithMaxWidth()`, `WithColumnWidths()`, `WithRowAutoWrap()`

## Example Algorithm

```go
func determineVisibleColumns(termWidth int, headers []string, minWidths []int) []int {
    visible := []int{}
    usedWidth := 0
    
    for i := 0; i < len(headers); i++ {
        minWidth := minWidths[i]
        borders := (len(visible) + 1) * 3 + 1
        
        if usedWidth + minWidth + borders <= termWidth {
            visible = append(visible, i)
            usedWidth += minWidth
        } else {
            break
        }
    }
    
    // Ensure at least one column
    if len(visible) == 0 {
        visible = []int{0}
    }
    
    return visible
}

func allocateColumnWidths(termWidth int, visibleCols []int, minWidths []int) map[int]int {
    widths := make(map[int]int)
    borders := (len(visibleCols) * 3) + 1
    
    // Fixed columns (all except last)
    fixedSpace := 0
    for i := 0; i < len(visibleCols)-1; i++ {
        colIdx := visibleCols[i]
        widths[colIdx] = minWidths[colIdx]
        fixedSpace += minWidths[colIdx]
    }
    
    // Dynamic column (last visible)
    lastIdx := visibleCols[len(visibleCols)-1]
    dynamicWidth := termWidth - fixedSpace - borders
    if dynamicWidth < 15 {
        dynamicWidth = 15
    }
    widths[lastIdx] = dynamicWidth
    
    return widths
}
```

## Testing Requirements

Add test cases for:
1. Full width (100 chars) - all columns visible
2. Progressive hiding (80, 60, 40, 25 chars)
3. Single column mode (20 chars)
4. Below minimum (10 chars) - responsive disabled
5. Long content truncation

## Files to Modify
- `pkg/output/types.go` - enhance TableOptions struct
- `pkg/output/table.go` - add responsive sizing logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TableOptions has DynamicColumn, MinColumnWidths, MinTerminalWidth fields
- [ ] TableBuilder has WithDynamicColumn(), WithMinColumnWidths() methods
- [ ] renderTable() implements column visibility algorithm
- [ ] renderTable() implements width allocation algorithm
- [ ] Text truncation with ellipsis works correctly
- [ ] Columns hide right-to-left as terminal narrows
- [ ] Single column mode works for narrow terminals
- [ ] Fallback mode works for extreme cases (<15 chars)
- [ ] Code is well-documented with inline comments
- [ ] No regressions in existing table rendering

## References
- Design specification: ai-config-manager-4fc notes (full 15-section specification)
- tablewriter v1.1.3 API: ai-config-manager-pjy notes
- API version fix: ai-config-manager-767
close_reason:
Implementation complete. All acceptance criteria met:

✅ Enhanced TableOptions with DynamicColumn, MinColumnWidths, MinTerminalWidth fields
✅ Added TableBuilder methods WithDynamicColumn() and WithMinColumnWidths()
✅ Implemented column visibility algorithm (determineVisibleColumns)
✅ Implemented width allocation algorithm (allocateColumnWidths)
✅ Text truncation with ellipsis using tw.WrapTruncate
✅ Columns hide right-to-left as terminal narrows
✅ Single column mode works for narrow terminals (<25 chars)
✅ Fallback mode for extreme cases (<15 chars)
✅ Well-documented code with inline comments
✅ No regressions - all existing tests pass

Implementation uses tablewriter v1.1.3 API correctly:
- NewTable() constructor with options
- WithMaxWidth() for terminal width constraint
- WithColumnWidths() for per-column width control
- WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate) for text truncation

Added comprehensive test coverage:
- 7 new test functions
- Updated measureColumnWidths() helper
- End-to-end responsive rendering tests
- All tests passing

Ready for migration task (ai-config-manager-3xk)

title: Audit: Find all table rendering locations in the project
description:
## Description
Identify all locations in the codebase where tables are rendered to ensure complete migration to the enhanced responsive table system.

## Instructions
1. Search for all usages of `output.NewTable()` across the codebase
2. Search for any direct tablewriter usage outside pkg/output
3. Document each location with:
   - File path and line number
   - Table headers/columns
   - Context (what data is being displayed)
   - Current column widths/behavior
   - Which column should be dynamic (typically description/last column)
4. Identify any special table rendering needs (custom formatting, etc.)
5. Create a comprehensive list in task notes

## Search Patterns
- `output.NewTable`
- `tablewriter.NewWriter`
- `.Format(output.Table)`
- Table rendering in cmd/ directory

## Expected Locations (verify completeness)
- cmd/list.go (repo list - NAME, DESCRIPTION)
- cmd/list_installed.go (installed resources - NAME, TARGETS, SYNC, DESCRIPTION)
- cmd/repo_import.go (import preview - #, NAME, DESCRIPTION)
- cmd/repo_verify.go (multiple tables for verification results)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Table columns identified for each usage
- [ ] Dynamic column identified for each table
- [ ] No table renderings missed

## Output Format

```
1. cmd/list.go:227
   - Columns: Name, Description
   - Dynamic column: Description

2. cmd/list_installed.go:321

```
notes:
## Table Rendering Audit - Complete Findings

### Summary
Found **7 distinct table rendering locations** across the codebase:
- 4 in cmd/ (user-facing commands)
- 2 in pkg/output/ (infrastructure)
- 1 special case (repo import selector)

### Direct tablewriter Usage (pkg/output/)

#### 1. pkg/output/formatter.go:194
**Location:** formatAsTable() function
**Purpose:** BulkOperationResult rendering for repo import operations
**Headers:** NAME, STATUS, MESSAGE
**Context:** Displays results of bulk import operations (added/updated/skipped/failed resources)
**Dynamic Column:** MESSAGE (last column) - contains error messages, status descriptions
**Special Notes:**
- Uses tablewriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout) directly
- Custom .Header() and .Append() methods (not output.NewTable)
- Already handles variable-length messages properly

#### 2. pkg/output/table.go:81
**Location:** renderTable() function
**Purpose:** Generic TableData rendering infrastructure
**Headers:** Dynamic (passed via TableData.Headers)
**Context:** Core table rendering used by all output.NewTable() calls
**Dynamic Column:** Last column by convention
**Special Notes:**
- Base infrastructure for all table rendering
- Converts headers to []any for tablewriter
- Has responsive sizing logic (currently placeholder)

### output.NewTable() Usage (cmd/)

#### 3. cmd/list.go:227
**Location:** outputWithPackagesTable() function
**Purpose:** Repository list command (aimgr repo list)
**Headers:** Name, Description
**Context:** Lists all resources in repository with type grouping (commands/skills/agents/packages)
**Dynamic Column:** Description (2nd/last column)
**Data:** Resource names, descriptions (truncated to 60 chars)
**Special Features:**
- AddSeparator() between resource types
- Package resources show count: "5 resources Processes PDF files..."

#### 4. cmd/list.go:281
**Location:** outputPackagesTable() function  
**Purpose:** Package-only list (aimgr repo list package/*)
**Headers:** Name, Resources, Description
**Context:** Lists packages with resource counts
**Dynamic Column:** Description (3rd/last column)
**Data:** Package name, resource count (number), description (truncated to 60 chars)

#### 5. cmd/list_installed.go:321
**Location:** outputInstalledTable() function
**Purpose:** Installed resources list (aimgr list)
**Headers:** Name, Targets, Sync, Description
**Context:** Shows installed resources with tool targets and sync status
**Dynamic Column:** Description (4th/last column)
**Data:**
- Name: "command/test", "skill/pdf-processing"
- Targets: "claude, opencode" (comma-separated)
- Sync: ✓ * ⚠ - (symbols)
- Description: Truncated to 40 chars
**Special Features:**
- AddSeparator() between resource types
- Legend printed after table

#### 6. cmd/repo_verify.go:459, 476, 493, 506, 523
**Location:** displayVerifyResults() function
**Purpose:** Repository verification (aimgr repo verify)
**Multiple Tables:**

a) **Resources without metadata** (line 459)
   - Headers: Type, Name, Status
   - Dynamic Column: Status (3rd/last)
   - Shows "Missing metadata" or "✓ Created metadata"

b) **Orphaned metadata** (line 476)
   - Headers: Type, Name, Status
   - Dynamic Column: Status (3rd/last)
   - Shows "Resource missing" or "✓ Removed metadata"

c) **Missing source paths** (line 493)
   - Headers: Type, Name, Source Path
   - Dynamic Column: Source Path (3rd/last)
   - Shows file paths

d) **Type mismatches** (line 506)
   - Headers: Name, Resource Type, Metadata Type
   - Dynamic Column: Metadata Type (3rd/last)
   - Shows resource type strings

e) **Packages with missing refs** (line 523)
   - Headers: Package, Missing Count, Missing Resources
   - Dynamic Column: Missing Resources (3rd/last)
   - Shows comma-separated list of missing resource refs

### Special Case: Interactive Selector

#### 7. cmd/repo_import.go:694
**Location:** selectResource() function
**Purpose:** Interactive resource selection when multiple matches found
**Headers:** #, Name, Description
**Context:** User prompt for disambiguation during import
**Dynamic Column:** Description (3rd/last column)
**Data:** Index number, resource name, description (truncated to 60 chars)
**Special Notes:**
- Not a typical output command
- Used in interactive prompts only
- Could benefit from responsive width

### Architecture Notes

**Two Rendering Paths:**
1. **output.NewTable()** → TableBuilder → TableData → renderTable() → tablewriter
   - Used by: cmd/list.go, cmd/list_installed.go, cmd/repo_verify.go, cmd/repo_import.go
   - Consistent API, goes through pkg/output/table.go
   
2. **Direct tablewriter** → tablewriter.NewWriter()
   - Used by: pkg/output/formatter.go (formatAsTable)
   - Custom .Header() and .Append() methods
   - Should migrate to output.NewTable() for consistency

### Dynamic Column Pattern

**100% consistency**: All tables use the **last column** as the dynamic column:
- Descriptions, messages, paths, status text
- Natural truncation behavior
- Aligns with user expectations

### Recommendations for Width-Aware Rendering

1. **Priority Order** (implement in this order):
   - High: cmd/list_installed.go (Description at 40 chars - most constrained)
   - High: cmd/list.go (Description at 60 chars)
   - Medium: cmd/repo_verify.go (Multiple tables, various dynamic columns)
   - Medium: cmd/repo_import.go (Interactive selector)
   - Low: pkg/output/formatter.go (MESSAGE column - usually short)

2. **Migration Path**:
   - Keep formatAsTable() as-is (uses custom Header/Append API)
   - Focus on output.NewTable() callers
   - Implement width detection in pkg/output/table.go:renderTable()
   - All output.NewTable() callers inherit automatically

3. **Terminal Width Distribution**:
   - Reserve space for fixed columns (Name, Targets, Sync, etc.)
   - Allocate remaining width to last column
   - Example: 80 chars terminal, 40 chars fixed → 40 chars for description
   - Example: 120 chars terminal, 40 chars fixed → 80 chars for description

### Files Requiring Changes

**No changes to these files** (when implementing width-aware):
- cmd/list.go ✓
- cmd/list_installed.go ✓
- cmd/repo_verify.go ✓
- cmd/repo_import.go ✓

**Only change needed:**
- pkg/output/table.go (renderTable function) - Add width calculation logic

This architecture allows **zero-change migration** for all table rendering locations.
close_reason:
Comprehensive audit complete. Found 7 distinct table rendering locations:

1. pkg/output/formatter.go:194 - BulkOperationResult (NAME, STATUS, MESSAGE)
2. pkg/output/table.go:81 - Generic TableData infrastructure
3. cmd/list.go:227 - Repository list (Name, Description)
4. cmd/list.go:281 - Package list (Name, Resources, Description)
5. cmd/list_installed.go:321 - Installed resources (Name, Targets, Sync, Description)
6. cmd/repo_verify.go - 5 verification tables (various columns)
7. cmd/repo_import.go:694 - Interactive selector (#, Name, Description)

Key findings:
- All tables use LAST COLUMN as dynamic column (100% consistent)
- Two rendering paths: output.NewTable() (preferred) and direct tablewriter
- Zero-change migration possible: only pkg/output/table.go needs updates
- Priority: list_installed.go (40 char truncation) and list.go (60 char truncation)

Full details documented in task notes with line numbers, context, and recommendations.

title: Research: Analyze tablewriter library capabilities for responsive sizing
description:
## Description
Research and document the tablewriter library's capabilities for implementing responsive column sizing, dynamic width allocation, and text truncation.

## Instructions

   - Setting column widths dynamically

3. Determine how to implement:
   - Full terminal width usage
   - Dynamic "stretch" column (typically last column)

4. Document findings in task notes including:
   - API methods to use
   - Limitations or workarounds needed
   - Recommended implementation approach

## Files to Review

notes:
# tablewriter v1.1.3 Research Findings

## Overview
The project uses tablewriter v1.1.3, which is a complete redesign from v0.0.5 with a configuration-driven API. The library provides extensive width control, wrapping, and truncation capabilities.

## Key API Methods for Responsive Column Sizing

### 1. Width Control Methods

#### Global Width Control:
- `WithMaxWidth(width int)` - Sets maximum total table width
- `WithColumnMax(width int)` - Sets global maximum column width (for all columns)

#### Per-Column Width Control:
- `WithColumnWidths(widths tw.Mapper[int, int])` - Sets specific width for each column
  - Example: `WithColumnWidths(map[int]int{0: 20, 1: 40, 2: 15})`
- `WithWidths(width tw.CellWidth)` - More comprehensive width config including Global and PerColumn

#### Section-Specific Width Control:
- `WithHeaderMaxWidth(maxWidth int)` - Max width for header cells
- `WithRowMaxWidth(maxWidth int)` - Max width for row cells  
- `WithFooterMaxWidth(maxWidth int)` - Max width for footer cells

### 2. Text Wrapping/Truncation Control

The library provides 4 wrapping modes via `tw.Wrap*` constants:

```go
const (
    WrapNone     = iota // No wrapping - text continues (may overflow)
    WrapNormal          // Standard word wrapping at spaces
    WrapTruncate        // Truncate text with ellipsis (...)
    WrapBreak           // Break words to fit (hard wrap)
)
```

#### Setting Wrap Modes:
- `WithHeaderAutoWrap(wrap int)` - Wrapping for headers
- `WithRowAutoWrap(wrap int)` - Wrapping for row cells
- `WithFooterAutoWrap(wrap int)` - Wrapping for footers

### 3. Column Hiding/Showing

- `WithAutoHide(state tw.State)` - Automatically hide columns with all empty cells
  - Use `tw.On` to enable, `tw.Off` to disable

### 4. Terminal Width Detection

The project already has terminal width detection in `pkg/output/terminal.go`:
- `NewTerminal()` - Gets current terminal dimensions
- `IsTTY()` - Checks if output is a terminal

## Implementation Approach

### Recommended Strategy for Responsive Tables:

```go
// 1. Detect terminal width
term, err := NewTerminal()
termWidth := 80 // default
if err == nil && term.Width() > 0 {
    termWidth = term.Width()
}

// 2. Calculate column widths dynamically
// Reserve space for borders: 3 chars per column (| col |) + 1 for final border
numCols := len(headers)
borderOverhead := (numCols * 3) + 1

// Allocate space for fixed-width columns (e.g., Type, Status)
fixedCols := map[int]int{
    0: 20,  // Name column
    2: 10,  // Status column
}

// Calculate remaining space for flexible column(s)
usedSpace := borderOverhead
for _, w := range fixedCols {
    usedSpace += w
}
flexibleSpace := termWidth - usedSpace

// 3. Create table with width constraints
table := tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout,
    tablewriter.WithMaxWidth(termWidth),
    tablewriter.WithColumnWidths(map[int]int{
        0: 20,                    // Fixed: Name
        1: flexibleSpace,         // Flexible: Description (gets remaining space)
        2: 10,                    // Fixed: Status
    }),
    tablewriter.WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate),  // Truncate with ...
)
```

### Alternative: ConfigBuilder Approach

For more complex scenarios:

```go
builder := tablewriter.NewConfigBuilder()
builder.WithMaxWidth(termWidth)
builder.Row().Formatting().WithAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate)
builder.ForColumn(0).WithWidth(20).Build()  // Name column
builder.ForColumn(1).WithWidth(flexibleSpace).Build()  // Description
builder.ForColumn(2).WithWidth(10).Build()  // Status

table := tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout, tablewriter.WithConfig(builder.Build()))
```

## Text Truncation with Ellipsis

To truncate text with "...", use:
```go
tablewriter.WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate)
```

This will automatically add "..." when text exceeds the column width.

## Column Visibility

To hide columns conditionally:
1. Use `WithAutoHide(tw.On)` for automatic hiding of empty columns
2. For manual control, filter data rows before adding to table
3. Adjust `headers` and data slices to exclude unwanted columns

## Limitations & Workarounds

### Limitation 1: No Dynamic "Stretch" Column Built-in
- **Issue**: Library doesn't have a built-in "fill remaining space" option for one column
- **Workaround**: Calculate manually using terminal width minus fixed columns (see code above)

### Limitation 2: WrapTruncate Behavior
- **Issue**: Truncation happens at max width, not at visible width
- **Workaround**: Set max width to desired truncation point minus 3 chars for "..."

### Limitation 3: Multi-line Headers with Truncation
- **Issue**: Truncation may not work well with multi-line wrapped headers
- **Workaround**: Use `WithHeaderAutoWrap(tw.WrapNone)` or fixed header text

### Limitation 4: Terminal Width Changes
- **Issue**: Table width is set once at creation, doesn't respond to terminal resize
- **Workaround**: Detect terminal width before each table render (current implementation)

## Code Examples

### Example 1: Basic Responsive Table
```go
func renderResponsiveTable(data *TableData) error {
    term, _ := NewTerminal()
    termWidth := term.Width()
    if termWidth == 0 {
        termWidth = 80
    }

    table := tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout,
        tablewriter.WithMaxWidth(termWidth),
        tablewriter.WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate),
    )
    
    table.Header(data.Headers)
    table.Bulk(convertRows(data.Rows))
    return table.Render()
}
```

### Example 2: Fixed + Flexible Columns
```go
func renderTableWithFlexColumn(headers []string, rows [][]string) error {
    term, _ := NewTerminal()
    termWidth := term.Width()
    
    // Fixed widths for first and last columns
    colWidths := calculateColumnWidths(termWidth, len(headers), 
        map[int]int{0: 20, 2: 10})
    
    table := tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout,
        tablewriter.WithMaxWidth(termWidth),
        tablewriter.WithColumnWidths(colWidths),
        tablewriter.WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate),
    )
    
    table.Header(headers)
    for _, row := range rows {
        table.Append(row...)
    }
    return table.Render()
}

func calculateColumnWidths(termWidth, numCols int, fixedCols map[int]int) map[int]int {
    borderOverhead := (numCols * 3) + 1
    usedSpace := borderOverhead
    
    for _, w := range fixedCols {
        usedSpace += w
    }
    
    flexibleWidth := (termWidth - usedSpace) / (numCols - len(fixedCols))
    
    result := make(map[int]int)
    for i := 0; i < numCols; i++ {
        if width, ok := fixedCols[i]; ok {
            result[i] = width
        } else {
            result[i] = flexibleWidth
        }
    }
    return result
}
```

### Example 3: Hide Columns Based on Terminal Width
```go
func renderAdaptiveTable(allHeaders []string, allRows [][]string) error {
    term, _ := NewTerminal()
    termWidth := term.Width()
    
    // Prioritize columns: required -> important -> optional
    columnsToShow := selectColumnsForWidth(termWidth, allHeaders)
    
    headers := filterColumns(allHeaders, columnsToShow)
    rows := make([][]string, len(allRows))
    for i, row := range allRows {
        rows[i] = filterColumns(row, columnsToShow)
    }
    
    table := tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout,
        tablewriter.WithMaxWidth(termWidth),
        tablewriter.WithRowAutoWrap(tw.WrapTruncate),
    )
    
    table.Header(headers)
    table.Bulk(rows)
    return table.Render()
}
```

## Migration Notes

The current implementation in `pkg/output/table.go` uses the legacy `NewWriter` approach but v1.1.3 is installed. Key changes needed:

1. **Constructor**: Change from `tablewriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout)` to `tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout, options...)`
2. **Headers**: Change from `table.SetHeader([]string)` to `table.Header(strings...)` or `table.Header([]string)`
3. **Rows**: `table.Append(row)` works the same, or use `table.Bulk([][]any)`
4. **Options**: Replace old `table.SetXxx()` calls with `WithXxx()` options in constructor

## References

- Library docs: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter@v1.1.3
- Migration guide: /home/hans/go/pkg/mod/github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter@v1.1.3/MIGRATION.md
- Wrap constants: /home/hans/go/pkg/mod/github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter@v1.1.3/tw/tw.go
- Options: /home/hans/go/pkg/mod/github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter@v1.1.3/option.go
close_reason:
Research complete. Documented tablewriter v1.1.3 API methods for:
- Width control (WithMaxWidth, WithColumnMax, WithColumnWidths)
- Text wrapping modes (WrapNone, WrapNormal, WrapTruncate, WrapBreak)
- Column hiding (WithAutoHide)
- Terminal width integration with existing pkg/output/terminal.go

Provided implementation approach with code examples for:
- Responsive tables using terminal width
- Dynamic column sizing (fixed + flexible columns)
- Text truncation with ellipsis
- Conditional column hiding based on space

Identified limitations and workarounds. Ready for implementation task.

title: Responsive table rendering with dynamic column sizing
description:
## Description
Enhance table rendering to be fully responsive and use the entire terminal width efficiently. Currently, tables don't utilize full screen width and lack smart column handling when space is limited.

## Goals
1. Tables should use the full terminal width available
2. One column (typically last) should be dynamically sized to fill remaining space
3. Text should be truncated with "..." when it doesn't fit
4. Columns should be removed entirely if insufficient space is available
5. All table usages in the project should benefit from this enhancement

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All tables use full terminal width
- [ ] Dynamic column sizing works correctly
- [ ] Text truncation with "..." works when needed
- [ ] Columns gracefully hide when space is too limited
- [ ] All existing table usages are migrated
- [ ] Tests verify the responsive behavior
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed

## Current State
- Basic responsive table infrastructure exists (terminal size detection, TTY checks)
- Tables use tablewriter library which has auto-wrap support
- Tables found in: cmd/list.go, cmd/list_installed.go, cmd/repo_import.go, cmd/repo_verify.go
- TableBuilder API is in place with WithResponsive() method

## Technical Context
- Terminal size detection: pkg/output/terminal.go
- Table rendering: pkg/output/table.go (renderTable function)
- Library: github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter
close_reason: Epic complete: All child tasks and acceptance gate closed. Responsive table rendering successfully implemented with full terminal width usage, dynamic column stretching, text truncation, graceful column hiding, 7 table locations migrated, 9 hardcoded truncations removed, 86+ test cases passing, comprehensive documentation. Feature is production-ready.

title: Add Windsurf IDE support
description:
## Description
Add support for Windsurf IDE as a new target platform in aimgr. Windsurf is a VSCode-based AI IDE with an agent called "Cascade" that supports skills (similar to Claude Code and OpenCode).

## Background
Windsurf uses:
- `.windsurf/skills/` for workspace-specific skills
- `~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/` for global skills
- Skills only (no commands or agents support)
- Same SKILL.md format as Claude/OpenCode (YAML frontmatter)

## Goals
- Users can install skills to Windsurf using `--tool=windsurf`
- Auto-detection works when `.windsurf/skills/` directory exists
- Multi-tool installation works (e.g., `--tool=claude,opencode,windsurf`)
- Commands and agents are properly blocked for Windsurf
- Comprehensive test coverage
- Updated documentation

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All child tasks completed
- [ ] All tests pass (unit + integration)
- [ ] Can install skills to `.windsurf/skills/`
- [ ] Detection works when `.windsurf/skills/` exists
- [ ] Commands and agents are blocked for Windsurf
- [ ] Multi-tool install works
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Acceptance gate passed

## Technical Notes
Implementation pattern is very similar to existing Copilot/VSCode support (skills-only, different directory structure).

## References
- Windsurf docs: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/skills.md
- Agent skills spec: https://agentskills.io
close_reason: All tasks and acceptance gate completed successfully. Windsurf IDE is now fully supported as the 4th AI coding tool. Implementation includes: tool type, parsing, configuration, detection, comprehensive tests (unit + integration), and complete documentation. All tests pass, code committed (7acef16) and pushed to remote.

title: Add git commit after modifying operations
description:
## Description
Add automatic git commits after operations that modify the repository (import, sync, remove). This makes all changes auditable.

## Instructions
1. Create helper function in `pkg/repo/manager.go`:
   - `CommitChanges(message string) error` - commits staged changes
   - Should check if git repo exists first
   - Use `git add .` to stage all changes (excluding .workspace via .gitignore)
   - Create commit with provided message
   - Return error if commit fails

2. Update operations to call CommitChanges():
   - `AddCommand`, `AddSkill`, `AddAgent`, `AddPackage` - after successful add
   - `AddBulk` - after bulk import completes
   - `Remove` - after successful removal
   - Use descriptive commit messages (e.g., "Add command: api/deploy", "Remove skill: pdf-processing")

3. Handle cases where git repo doesn't exist:
   - Operations should work even if not a git repo (backward compat)
   - Log warning if CommitChanges fails but don't fail the operation

## Files to Modify
- `pkg/repo/manager.go` - add CommitChanges method and call it in operations
- May need to update tests to handle git operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CommitChanges() method implemented
- [ ] All add operations create commits
- [ ] Bulk import creates commit
- [ ] Remove operation creates commit
- [ ] Operations work even if not a git repo
- [ ] .workspace/ is not tracked in git
- [ ] Commit messages are descriptive
- [ ] Tests pass (may need to mock git or skip if not initialized)

## Notes
- Use os/exec for git commands
- Commit message format: "aimgr: <operation> <resource-type>: <name>"
- Example: "aimgr: add skill: pdf-processing"
- Example: "aimgr: remove command: api/deploy"
- For bulk: "aimgr: import 5 resources (3 skills, 2 commands)"
close_reason: Implemented CommitChanges method and integrated it into AddBulk and Remove operations

title: FINDINGS: agent-skills-cli uses agentskills.in API, NOT skillsmp.com
description:
## Critical Finding: Clarifying the Confusion

### What `skills market-list` Actually Does

**Answer**: It calls `https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills` (NOT skillsmp.com)

### The Evidence

**From source code** (`/tmp/agent-skills-cli/src/core/skillsdb.ts`):
```typescript
const SKILLS_API = typeof __SKILLS_API_URL__ !== 'undefined'
    ? __SKILLS_API_URL__
    : 'https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills';
```

**Search/Install Commands**:
- `skills search` → agentskills.in API
- `skills install @author/name` → agentskills.in API
- `skills market-list` → agentskills.in API

### What About skillsmp.com?

**skillsmp.com is mentioned in:**
1. **CHANGELOG.md**: "Access to 40,779+ skills from skillsmp.com"
2. **Source code comments**: `// GitHub URL Helpers (moved from skillsmp.ts)`
3. **One marketplace command**: Preview URLs (https://skillsmp.com/skills/...)

**BUT**: The actual API calls go to agentskills.in, NOT skillsmp.com

### What is `skills market-sources`?

**Answer**: Lists **GitHub repositories** configured as marketplace sources.

**From source code** (`src/types/marketplace.ts`):
```typescript
export const DEFAULT_MARKETPLACES: MarketplaceSource[] = [
    {
        id: 'anthropic-skills',
        name: 'Anthropic Official Skills',
        owner: 'anthropics',
        repo: 'skills',
        branch: 'main',
        skillsPath: 'skills',
        description: 'Official Agent Skills from Anthropic',
        verified: true
    }
];
```

**The output you saw**:
```
Primary Marketplace:
  SkillsMP ✓
    URL: https://skillsmp.com
    Skills: 40,000+

Legacy GitHub Sources:
  Anthropic Official Skills ✓
    Repo: anthropics/skills
```

**This is misleading UI** - "SkillsMP" is just a label, but the backend calls agentskills.in API!

### The Architecture

```
agent-skills-cli
    ↓
agentskills.in/api/skills  ← ACTUAL API
    ↓
GitHub repos (175k+ skills crawled)
```

**NOT**:
```
agent-skills-cli → skillsmp.com/api  ← DOES NOT EXIST
```

### Key Points

1. **agentskills.in = THE API** (175k skills)
2. **skillsmp.com = ???** (40k skills mentioned, but no API used)
3. **Possible explanation**:
   - skillsmp.com might be an older project/domain
   - agentskills.in replaced it
   - CLI still has legacy references but doesn't use it

### Relationship Between Them

**Hypothesis 1**: Same project, rebrand
- skillsmp.com → agentskills.in
- Old branding still in CLI output for "recognition"

**Hypothesis 2**: Different projects, competition
- skillsmp.com = competitor (40k skills)
- agentskills.in = newer, larger (175k skills)
- CLI switched to agentskills.in

**Hypothesis 3**: Confusing naming
- "SkillsMP" in UI = generic label for "marketplace"
- Not referencing skillsmp.com domain

### What This Means for aimgr

**The agent-skills-cli integration we were considering**:
- ✅ Uses agentskills.in API (confirmed)
- ❌ Does NOT use skillsmp.com API
- ✅ Falls back to GitHub direct access for marketplace sources

**Our previous decision stands**:
- No need to integrate agentskills.in API
- Users can already use `gh:` prefix
- API is just discovery layer on GitHub

### Action Items

- [ ] Verify if skillsmp.com even has an API (manual browser check needed)
- [ ] Determine relationship between skillsmp.com and agentskills.in
- [ ] Update research docs with this clarification

### Blocker

Cannot access skillsmp.com via automation (Cloudflare protection).
Needs manual browser investigation.
close_reason: Research complete: Can access crawled data via API only, no bulk dump available. API supports pagination up to 1000 skills per request. 175k skills = ~176 API calls needed to get all data.

title: Investigate skillsmp.com marketplace
description:
## Context

User identified https://skillsmp.com as another AI skills marketplace that was missed in the initial research.

## Investigation Blocked

**Current Issue**: Site is behind Cloudflare bot protection
- Returns 403 for automated requests
- Blocks curl, webfetch, etc.
- Needs manual browser access

## Research Questions

1. **What is skillsmp.com?**
   - Who operates it?
   - When was it launched?
   - What's the relationship to agentskills.in (same team? competitor?)

2. **Scale & Content**
   - How many skills indexed?
   - Quality/curation approach?
   - Active maintenance?

3. **API Access**
   - Does it have a public API?
   - API endpoints? (e.g., /api/skills)
   - Rate limits? Authentication?

4. **Comparison with agentskills.in**
   - Feature parity?
   - Unique offerings?
   - Which is more comprehensive?

5. **Integration Strategy**
   - Should we support both?
   - Is one a subset of the other?
   - Can we unify the search interface?

## Investigation Steps

### Step 1: Manual Browser Visit
```bash
# From browser (not CLI):
# 1. Visit https://skillsmp.com
# 2. Screenshot homepage
# 3. Check for API docs
# 4. Search for a skill (e.g., "pdf")
# 5. Note navigation, features, UX
```

### Step 2: Compare With AgentSkills.in

| Feature | skillsmp.com | agentskills.in |
|---------|--------------|----------------|
| Skill Count | ??? | 175,737 |
| API | ??? | ✅ Yes |
| Categories | ??? | 14 |
| Search | ??? | ✅ Full-text |
| GitHub Integration | ??? | ✅ Automated |

### Step 3: Community Research
- Search GitHub for "skillsmp.com" references
- Check Reddit/Discord discussions
- Look for integration examples
- Find official documentation

### Step 4: Technical Investigation
```bash
# If API exists, test it:
curl "https://skillsmp.com/api/skills"
curl "https://skillsmp.com/api/skills?search=pdf"

# Check for API docs:
# - /api/docs
# - /swagger
# - /openapi.json
```

## Expected Output

Create document: `docs/research/skillsmp-comparison.md`

**Contents:**
1. **Overview**: What is skillsmp.com?
2. **Features**: Documented capabilities
3. **API**: Endpoints, authentication, rate limits
4. **Scale**: Skill count, categories, coverage
5. **Comparison Table**: skillsmp.com vs agentskills.in
6. **Integration Recommendation**: 
   - Support both?
   - Pick one?
   - Unified abstraction?

## Why This Was Missed

**Research Methodology Gaps:**
1. ❌ Only searched GitHub topics (incomplete)
2. ❌ Only checked docs/research/ (internal knowledge)
3. ❌ Didn't do broad web search ("AI skill marketplace")
4. ❌ Didn't check alternative TLDs

**Lesson Learned:**
- GitHub != complete picture
- Need multi-source validation
- User/community knowledge is critical
- Always ask: "What am I missing?"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Successfully accessed skillsmp.com (manual browser visit)
- [ ] Documented features, UI, and capabilities
- [ ] Confirmed API existence/absence
- [ ] Compared with agentskills.in (feature-by-feature)
- [ ] Created comparison document
- [ ] Updated integration strategy in ai-config-manager-vos
- [ ] Recommended path forward (support both/one/neither)

## Blocker

**Cannot proceed via CLI/automation** - Cloudflare blocks bot access.

**Required**: Manual investigation from web browser.

## Related Tasks

- ai-config-manager-vos: AgentSkills API Integration
- ai-config-manager-uk8: Other AI Skill Registries (parent)

## Apology & Thanks

Thank you for catching this! It's a significant oversight that could have led to an incomplete integration strategy. This is exactly the kind of validation that prevents building the wrong thing.
close_reason: User decision: Cancel AgentSkills integration. No search feature wanted. AgentSkills.in is just a GitHub crawler - users can already use gh: prefix directly. Not worth the complexity.

title: Plan: AgentSkills API Integration Strategy
description:
## Key Discovery

✅ **AgentSkills API EXISTS** at `https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills`
❌ **Currently returning HTTP 500** - Server error, not 404
✅ **This means the endpoint is implemented** but having issues

## API Test Results

```bash
$ curl https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills
{"error":"Internal Server Error"}

$ curl -w "\nHTTP Status: %{http_code}\n" https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills  
{"error":"Internal Server Error"}
HTTP Status: 500
```

## Integration Strategy

Since the API exists but is unstable, we should:

### Phase 1: Git-Based Integration (Immediate)
- Use existing `gh:owner/repo` syntax
- Works TODAY with zero new code
- Example: `aimgr repo import gh:anthropic/skills`

### Phase 2: API Integration (When stable)
- Add `as:@owner/skill` prefix
- Resolves via AgentSkills API
- Falls back to Git if API unavailable
- Example: `aimgr repo import as:@anthropic/xlsx`

## Proposed Implementation

### 1. Add Resilient API Client
```go
// pkg/source/agentskills/client.go
type Client struct {
    BaseURL string
    Timeout time.Duration
}

func (c *Client) ResolveSkill(owner, name string) (*SkillInfo, error) {
    // Try API first
    info, err := c.fetchFromAPI(owner, name)
    if err != nil {
        // Fallback to GitHub convention
        return c.resolveViaGitHub(owner, name)
    }
    return info, nil
}
```

### 2. Parse `as:` Prefix
```go
// pkg/source/parser.go
if strings.HasPrefix(input, "as:") {
    return parseAgentSkillsPrefix(input)
}

func parseAgentSkillsPrefix(input string) (*ParsedSource, error) {
    // Parse: as:@anthropic/xlsx
    // Use API client to resolve
    // Convert to GitHub URL if API fails
}
```

### 3. Graceful Degradation
```
User: aimgr repo import as:@anthropic/xlsx
  ↓
Try: API lookup (agentskills.in/api/skills)
  ↓ (if fails)
Fallback: GitHub convention (gh:anthropic/skills)
  ↓
Import skill
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Can import via `gh:owner/repo` (works today)
- [ ] Can import via `as:@owner/skill` (new feature)
- [ ] API client handles HTTP 500 gracefully
- [ ] Falls back to GitHub if API unavailable
- [ ] Error messages guide users to working alternatives
- [ ] Documented: When to use `gh:` vs `as:` prefix

## Next Steps

1. **Document existing Git support** - Show users `gh:` works now
2. **Design API client** - Resilient, with fallback
3. **Wait for API stability** - Or contact AgentSkills team
4. **Implement `as:` prefix** - When API is reliable

## References

- Research: `docs/research/source-provider-architecture.md`
- Investigation: ai-config-manager-dgh
close_reason: AgentSkills.in is Git-based, not API-based. No special integration needed - already supported via gh: prefix for GitHub repos.

title: Research: Metadata tracking for agentskills.in sources
description:
## Description

Research how to track source metadata for skills imported from agentskills.in, ensuring we can identify their origin and check for updates.

## Research Questions

1. **What metadata do we currently track?**
   - For GitHub sources: What's stored?
   - For local sources: What's stored?
   - Where is metadata stored? (in resource files? separate .metadata.yaml?)

2. **What agentskills.in metadata should we track?**
   - API provides:
     - `id` (unique skill ID)
     - `name` (skill name)
     - `author` (author username)
     - `scopedName` (@author/name)
     - `githubUrl` (source repo URL)
     - `rawUrl` (direct download URL)
     - `stars`, `forks` (popularity)
     - `category`
     - `updatedAt` (last update timestamp)

   - Which fields are essential?
   - Which enable update checking?

3. **How to handle scoped names?**
   - agentskills uses `@author/name` format
   - aimgr uses `skill/name` format (no namespacing)
   - Options:
     - A: Store as `skills/pdf-processing/` (drop author)
     - B: Store as `skills/@anthropic/pdf-processing/` (preserve namespace)
     - C: Store as `skills/pdf-processing/` but track author in metadata

4. **How to handle name conflicts?**
   - Multiple authors might have skills with same name
   - Example: Both `@anthropic/pdf` and `@vercel/pdf` exist
   - How to differentiate in local repo?

5. **Update checking mechanism:**
   - GitHub: Can we `git fetch` and compare commits?
   - agentskills: Query API by ID, compare `updatedAt`?
   - Do we need to store last-synced timestamp?

## Investigation Steps

1. **Review existing metadata:**
   ```bash
   # Check skills in repository
   find ~/.local/share/ai-config/repo/skills -name "*.yaml" -o -name ".metadata*"
   
   # Check resource structs
   grep -r "Metadata" pkg/resource/
   ```

2. **Analyze resource loading:**
   - How does `LoadSkill()` read metadata?
   - Is source URL stored with resources?
   - Can we retrieve source after import?

3. **Check existing source tracking:**
   - Does GitHub URL get stored?
   - Can we tell if a skill came from GitHub vs local?

4. **Design metadata schema:**
   ```yaml
   # Proposed: .metadata.yaml in skill directory
   name: pdf-processing
   type: skill
   source:
     provider: agentskills      # "local", "github", "agentskills"
     
     # agentskills-specific fields:
     id: skill-abc123
     scopedName: "@anthropic/pdf-processing"
     author: anthropic
     githubUrl: "https://github.com/anthropics/skills/..."
     rawUrl: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/..."
     stars: 450
     category: documents
     
   imported:
     at: "2026-02-07T10:30:00Z"
     by: "aimgr v0.2.0"
   
   updated:
     at: "2026-02-07T10:30:00Z"
     apiVersion: "2026-02-07T08:00:00Z"  # updatedAt from API
   ```

## Expected Output

Create section in: `docs/research/source-provider-architecture.md`

**Contents:**
1. **Current Metadata Tracking**
   - What we track for GitHub sources
   - What we track for local sources
   - Where metadata is stored

2. **Proposed Metadata Schema**
   - Fields for agentskills sources
   - Rationale for each field

3. **Name Conflict Resolution**
   - Recommendation for handling scoped names
   - Examples of conflict scenarios
   - Proposed solution

4. **Update Detection Strategy**
   - How to detect when skill has updates
   - Comparison with GitHub approach

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Documented current metadata tracking approach
- [ ] Proposed metadata schema for agentskills sources
- [ ] Decided on scoped name handling (preserve author or not?)
- [ ] Designed update detection mechanism
- [ ] Addressed name conflict scenarios
- [ ] Schema is extensible for future providers
close_reason: AgentSkills.in is Git-based, not API-based. No special integration needed - already supported via gh: prefix for GitHub repos.

title: Research: Workspace caching strategy for agentskills.in
description:
## Description

Investigate whether workspace caching (like we use for GitHub repos) is beneficial for agentskills.in sources, or if we should use a different approach.

## Context

**GitHub sources**: We clone Git repos to `~/.local/share/ai-config/repo/.workspace/` for 10-50x faster subsequent imports.

**agentskills.in**: Provides full skill content via API response (`content` field in JSON). No Git clone needed.

## Research Questions

1. **Does agentskills.in need workspace caching?**
   - API returns full content in response
   - No need to clone Git repos
   - But: API calls have latency, could we cache responses?

2. **If we cache, what do we cache?**
   - Option A: Cache API responses (JSON) for 5 minutes
   - Option B: Store downloaded skills in workspace-like directory
   - Option C: No caching, always fetch fresh from API

3. **How would workspace be used?**
   - GitHub: `workspace/github.com-owner-repo-ref/`
   - agentskills: `workspace/agentskills-<skill-id>/`?
   - Or: No workspace at all?

4. **What's the update flow?**
   - GitHub: `git pull` in cached repo
   - agentskills: Re-fetch from API (always fresh)
   - Do we need to cache for `aimgr repo sync` performance?

5. **Comparison with GitHub source:**
   ```
   GitHub source:
     1. Parse gh:owner/repo
     2. workspace.GetOrClone(url, ref)
     3. Discovery in workspace path
     4. Import from workspace
   
   agentskills source:
     1. Parse agentskills:@author/skill
     2. API call to fetch skill
     3. ??? Where to put downloaded content ???
     4. Discovery from ???
     5. Import from ???
   ```

## Investigation Steps

1. **Review workspace architecture:**
   - Read `pkg/workspace/manager.go`
   - Understand caching benefits (performance, offline use)
   - Check pruning logic

2. **Analyze GitHub source flow:**
   - How does it use workspace?
   - When does it populate workspace?
   - When does it update workspace?

3. **Consider agentskills scenarios:**
   - **Scenario A**: No workspace
     - Fetch from API → temp dir → import → delete temp
     - Pros: Simple, always fresh
     - Cons: No caching, repeated API calls

   - **Scenario B**: Workspace for skills
     - Fetch from API → `workspace/agentskills-<id>/` → import
     - Pros: Caching, offline after first fetch
     - Cons: More complex, might be stale

   - **Scenario C**: Hybrid
     - Use GitHub URL from API → workspace cache GitHub repo
     - Pros: Leverage existing workspace
     - Cons: Extra step, not using API content

## Expected Output

Create section in: `docs/research/source-provider-architecture.md`

**Contents:**
1. **Workspace Benefits Analysis**
   - Performance improvement numbers
   - Offline use cases
   - Disk space considerations

2. **Recommendation for agentskills**
   - Use workspace? Yes/No
   - If yes: How to structure it?
   - If no: Alternative approach?

3. **Implementation sketch**
   - Pseudocode for agentskills source resolution
   - Where content lives during import
   - Cleanup strategy

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Documented how workspace caching works for GitHub
- [ ] Analyzed 3 scenarios for agentskills (no cache, workspace, hybrid)
- [ ] Provided clear recommendation with rationale
- [ ] Sketched implementation approach
- [ ] Considered `aimgr repo sync` performance implications
close_reason: AgentSkills.in is Git-based, not API-based. No special integration needed - already supported via gh: prefix for GitHub repos.

title: Research: Analyze source provider architecture and interfaces
description:
## Description

Analyze current codebase to understand how source providers (local, GitHub, Git) are implemented. Document the architecture to inform agentskills.in integration.

## Research Questions

title: AgentSkills.in Marketplace Integration
description:
## Description

Integrate agentskills.in marketplace as a source provider for aimgr, enabling users to discover and import skills from their 100,000+ skill catalog.

## Goals

- **Discovery**: Users can search agentskills.in marketplace from aimgr CLI
- **Import**: Skills from agentskills.in can be imported to local repository
- **Sync**: agentskills.in sources work with `aimgr repo sync` config
- **Backend Architecture**: Clean abstraction for agentskills as a source provider (similar to GitHub)

## Strategic Position

agentskills.in is **complementary**, not a competitor:
- **They provide**: Discovery layer (marketplace, search, 100k+ skills)
- **We provide**: Local repository management, project integration, multi-source sync

## User Workflow

1. **Decide which skills to use** → Configure sources from multiple providers:
   ```yaml
   sync:
     sources:
       - url: ~/dev/my-skills              # Local
       - url: gh:anthropics/skills         # GitHub
       - url: agentskills:@anthropic/xlsx  # AgentSkills marketplace
   ```

2. **Use in projects** → Install from local repository:
   ```bash
   aimgr install skill/pdf-processing
   ```

3. **Update cleanly** → Via unified sync:
   ```bash
   aimgr repo sync  # Updates ALL sources
   ```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Can import skills from agentskills.in: `aimgr repo import agentskills:@anthropic/xlsx`
- [ ] agentskills sources work in sync config
- [ ] Backend has clean source abstraction (interface/trait pattern)
- [ ] Metadata tracks agentskills.in sources properly
- [ ] Workspace caching strategy decided (use or skip?)
- [ ] Architecture documented for future source providers

## Non-Goals (Future Phases)

- Search command (`aimgr search agentskills python`) - separate epic
- Interactive skill selection UI - separate epic
- Category browsing - separate epic
- Lock file / update checking - separate epic

## Architecture Questions to Answer

1. Do we have clear source provider interfaces?
2. How does GitHub source work? Can we follow same pattern?
3. Is workspace caching useful for agentskills? (they provide full content via API)
4. How to track metadata for agentskills sources?
5. How to handle scoped names (@author/skill)?

## References

- Competitor analysis: `docs/research/competitor-analysis-agent-skills-cli.md`
- Integration plan: `docs/research/agentskills-integration-plan.md`
- agentskills.in API: `https://www.agentskills.in/api/skills`
close_reason: Research confirmed AgentSkills.in is a directory of GitHub repos with no programmatic API. Our existing gh: prefix support already handles this use case. No new backend integration needed.

title: Document repo override-source edge-case policy
description:
## Summary
Acceptance review for ai-config-manager-ukp2.4 found that shipped CLI behavior and automated tests enforce two edge-case policies, but the user-facing docs do not state them explicitly.

## Observed runtime behavior
Using the local binary in isolated temp repos:
- A second override attempt on an already-overridden source fails with:
  - `source "team-tools" is already overridden ... Run 'aimgr repo override-source team-tools --clear' first`
- Attempting to override a source that is already local fails with:
  - `source "local-only" is already a local path source ... overriding already-local sources is not supported`

## Why this is a bug
Acceptance criteria for ai-config-manager-ukp2.4 require double-override and already-local-source behavior to match the documented policy, and docs/tests to match shipped CLI behavior. The tests cover these policies, but docs such as `docs/user-guide/sources.md`, `docs/user-guide/getting-started.md`, and/or troubleshooting do not document them.

## Expected
Docs should explicitly state the final supported policy for:
1. Double-override attempts (currently rejected; user must clear first)
2. Override attempts on already-local sources (currently unsupported)

## Evidence
- Command tests: `go test ./cmd -run 'TestRepoOverrideSource' -count=1`
- Manual verification with `./aimgr` in temp repos reproduced both errors.
close_reason: Updated user docs to explicitly document override-source double-override rejection and already-local-source unsupported policy, including clear-first recovery guidance.

title: TestRepoRemove_GitCommit fails: test setup doesn't commit .metadata/sources.json created by manifest.Save()
description:
## Summary
`TestRepoRemove_GitCommit` fails because the test setup leaves `.metadata/sources.json` untracked after `manifest.Save()` now persists source metadata. The test expects a clean baseline except for `M unrelated.txt`, but instead sees unexpected untracked `.metadata/` state.

## Likely Root Cause
`manifest.Save()` now writes `.metadata/sources.json` as part of source metadata persistence. In `cmd/repo_remove_test.go`, the initial git setup commit still commits only `ai.repo.yaml`, so later `git status --short` includes untracked `.metadata/`.

## Scope
This is a test setup bug, not a production behavior bug.

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/repo_remove_test.go`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `go test ./cmd -run TestRepoRemove_GitCommit -count=1` passes.
- [ ] Other tests in `cmd/repo_remove_test.go` still pass.
- [ ] The fix keeps the test intent intact by ensuring setup commits all files created by manifest persistence.
close_reason: Fixed test setup to commit .metadata/sources.json created by manifest.Save(), restoring clean git baseline for TestRepoRemove_GitCommit

description: Research whether aimgr should support a switch-source or override-source command that lets users temporarily switch a named source between local (symlink) and remote (copy) without the current multi-step workaround. Deliverable: critical review answering 7 architectural questions.

title: Release docs versioning command misses scoped conventional commits
description:
## Summary
The version-bump guidance in `docs/contributor-guide/release-process.md` does not detect scoped Conventional Commit subjects such as `feat(resource): ...` or `fix(repo): ...`.

## Reproduction
- `git log v3.5.0..HEAD --pretty=format:"%s" | rg "^(feat|fix|BREAKING):"`

## Observed
The command returns no matches on current main even though commits since `v3.5.0` include:
- `feat(resource): add resource validation workflow`
- `fix(repo): preserve invalid package refs in verify`
- `feat(manifest): add local overlays and harden shared team manifests`

## Impact
Release operators following the documented command can miss semver-significant commits and misclassify the required release bump.
close_reason: Updated release-process versioning guidance to detect scoped conventional commits

title: Docs still describe shared-manifest ref updates as conflicts
description:
## Summary
The shared-manifest docs still say that same-name sources with different definitions conflict, explicitly listing `ref` in that definition, even though apply-manifest now updates the ref when the canonical source matches.

## Why this matters
Teams using pinned or tagged shared manifests need accurate guidance for routine manifest updates. The current docs can mislead users into thinking a normal ref bump should fail.

## Evidence
- `pkg/repomanifest/apply_merge.go` now updates `Ref` and reports `updated source ref` for same-name canonical sources.
- `pkg/repomanifest/apply_merge_test.go` includes `TestMergeForApply_UpdatesRefWhenCanonicalSourceMatches` and `TestMergeForApply_UpdatesRefAndPreservesIncludeInPreserveMode`.
- `cmd/repo_apply_test.go` includes `TestRepoApply_ReapplyUpdatesSourceRef`.
- Docs still say:
  - `docs/user-guide/sources.md`: `Same source name + different definition (path/url/ref/subpath/include) -> conflict`
  - `docs/user-guide/team-workflows.md`: `same source name + different definition -> explicit failure` and troubleshooting lists different `ref` as a conflict symptom.

## Expected
Docs should distinguish between:
- same canonical source with updated ref -> update supported
- genuinely different source location/identity -> conflict

## Actual
Current docs overstate conflicts and contradict implemented ref-update behavior.
close_reason: Updated shared-manifest docs to reflect supported same-canonical-source ref updates and clarify true source conflicts.

title: Shared manifest re-apply leaves removed sources without team-workflow guidance
description:
## Summary
When a team republishes a shared ai.repo.yaml without a previously included source, re-applying the new manifest leaves the old source in the local repo. The shared-manifest workflow docs do not explain this additive behavior or how teams should clean up stale sources.

## Reproduction
1. Apply manifest m1 containing sources source-a and source-b.
2. Re-apply manifest m2 containing only source-b.
3. Run ./aimgr repo show-manifest.

## Expected
The workflow should be explicitly defined for teams. Either the removed source should be cleaned up automatically, or the docs should clearly say that apply-manifest is additive and that users must run repo drop-source for stale entries.

## Actual
source-a remains in ai.repo.yaml after re-applying m2, and the central shared-manifest workflow docs do not call out this stale-source cleanup step.

## Evidence
Verified with temp repos using ./aimgr only; after applying m2, repo show-manifest still listed both source-a and source-b.
close_reason: Documented additive apply-manifest behavior and stale-source cleanup via repo drop-source

title: verify reports overlay-only resources as coming from ai.package.yaml
description:

title: Deduplicate Init() directory creation stanzas in repo/manager.go
description:
## Summary

`pkg/repo/manager.go` `Init()` method (lines 170-258, already marked `//nolint:gocyclo`) contains 5 nearly identical directory-creation blocks. Each block:

1. Checks if directory exists
2. Creates it with `os.MkdirAll`
3. Logs success
4. Handles error with wrapped message

The only difference between blocks is the directory path and the log/error message string.

## Impact

- Readability: 90 lines of repetitive code obscure the actual initialization logic
- Maintainability: changes to the creation pattern (e.g., adding permissions checks) require 5 identical edits
- The `//nolint:gocyclo` would likely become unnecessary after this refactor

## Suggested fix

Extract a helper:
```go
func ensureDir(path, label string) error {
    if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
        if err := os.MkdirAll(path, 0o755); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("creating %s directory: %w", label, err)
        }
        slog.Debug("Created directory", "type", label, "path", path)
    }
    return nil
}
```

Then Init() becomes ~10 lines of `ensureDir()` calls. Ask-first — the nolint directive and logging format may be intentional choices worth discussing.
close_reason: Replaced 5 identical dir-creation blocks with data-driven loop in Init()

title: Deduplicate HasSource() logic in repo/remove.go
description:
## Summary

`pkg/repo/remove.go` `HasSource()` method (lines 115-188) contains duplicated matching logic between the logged (`m.verbose`) and non-logged branches. The entire source-matching loop (iterate sources, check name/URL/path match) is written twice — once with debug logging and once without.

## Impact

- Maintainability: any change to matching logic must be made in two places
- Bug risk: the two branches can silently drift out of sync

## Suggested fix

Extract the matching logic into a helper that returns whether a match was found, and optionally collect log messages or use a callback for logging. Something like:

```go
func (m *Manager) matchesSource(source Source, nameOrURL string) bool {
    // single implementation of matching logic
}
```

Then HasSource() becomes a thin loop calling matchesSource() with optional logging. Safe to auto-apply — no behavior change, just structural refactor.
close_reason: Deduplicated HasSource() by computing match variables once, logging conditionally

title: Compile regexps at package level in repomanifest
description:
## Summary

`pkg/repomanifest/manifest.go` compiles regexps on every function call in two places:

1. `isValidSourceName()` (line 349): `regexp.MustCompile("^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$")`
2. `generateSourceName()` (line 388): `regexp.MustCompile("[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]+")` and (line 400): `regexp.MustCompile("^[^a-zA-Z0-9]+")`

These should be package-level `var` declarations using `regexp.MustCompile` — compiled once at init time. This is a well-known Go pattern and is called out as a lint issue by most Go linters.

## Impact

- Performance: unnecessary repeated compilation on every call
- Style: violates standard Go convention for static regexps

## Suggested fix

Add at package level:
```go
var (
    validSourceNameRe  = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$`)
    invalidCharsRe     = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]+`)
    leadingInvalidRe   = regexp.MustCompile(`^[^a-zA-Z0-9]+`)
)
```

Then reference these vars in the functions. Safe to auto-apply — no behavior change.
close_reason: Compiled regexps at package level: validSourceNameRe and invalidNameCharRe

title: Document atomic-write model for repo-managed state files
description:

2. Cover at least these files/areas:

   - resource metadata under `.metadata/...`
   - `.workspace/.cache-metadata.json`

   - temp file in same directory
   - file sync
   - rename replacement
   - parent directory sync where supported
4. Ensure the documentation does not overstate guarantees beyond what the implementation actually provides.
5. Keep locking and atomic-write descriptions consistent across all updated docs.

title: Scope git staging and commits for repo mutations
description:
## Description
Harden git staging/commit behavior so concurrent or adjacent repo changes are not accidentally swept into the wrong commit, and commit boundaries align with the actual operation being performed.

## Instructions
1. Review `pkg/repo/git.go` commit behavior and eliminate broad `git add .` staging for repo mutation commits.
2. Stage only the files intentionally changed by each operation, or refactor commit helpers so the caller supplies an explicit path set.
3. Ensure commit helpers behave correctly while the repo lock is held and do not create additional race windows between staging, status inspection, and commit.
4. Update mutating operations that currently rely on broad commit behavior (`add`, `remove`, `sync`, `apply-manifest`, and related flows).
5. Keep existing non-git-repo graceful behavior intact.

## Files to Modify
- pkg/repo/git.go
- pkg/repo/add.go
- pkg/repo/remove.go
- pkg/repo/bulk.go
- cmd/repo_add.go
- cmd/repo_sync.go
- cmd/repo_remove.go
- cmd/repo_apply.go
- any related helpers that determine which files changed during an operation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Repo mutation commits no longer use blanket `git add .`.
- [ ] Each operation commits only the files it intentionally changed.
- [ ] Git commits remain correct when repo lock coverage is enabled.
- [ ] Tests cover commit scoping for at least add/remove/sync manifest updates.
- [ ] `make test` passes.
close_reason: Implemented scoped staging and commit paths

title: Update docs and AI resource manager references for owned-folder cleanup
description:
## Description
Refresh user-facing docs, troubleshooting, changelog notes, and bundled AI guidance so they describe the new folder-ownership model and revised command semantics accurately.

## Instructions
1. Remove or rewrite references to `aimgr repair --reset`, `aimgr repair --force`, `aimgr clean --yes`, and the old managed/unmanaged/orphan repair model.
2. Explain that `aimgr clean` empties owned resource directories and that `aimgr repair` reconciles them to `ai.package.yaml`.
3. Document the migration path: `aimgr clean && aimgr repair` replaces the old `aimgr repair --reset --force` workflow.
4. Document that `aimgr repair` keeps `--dry-run` and `--prune-package`, and explain that `--prune-package` is separate manifest cleanup rather than folder reconciliation.
5. Document that `aimgr repair` will reinstall declared resources that were manually removed; explain that permanent removal requires updating `ai.package.yaml` (for example via `aimgr uninstall` without `--no-save`).
6. Update examples, troubleshooting steps, command references, and any structured output documentation impacted by verify/repair/clean output changes.
7. Add upgrade notes for the breaking CLI behavior changes and JSON/output terminology changes where appropriate.
8. Audit `docs/user-guide/concepts.md` and `ai-resources/skills/ai-resource-manager/SKILL.md` early to determine whether they need conceptual rewrites, not just spot edits.
9. Audit additional docs/help references discovered during implementation, including `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and other command-reference pages if they mention removed flags or old terminology.

## Files to Modify
- `docs/user-guide/repair.md`
- `docs/user-guide/getting-started.md`
- `docs/user-guide/README.md`
- `docs/user-guide/concepts.md`
- `docs/reference/troubleshooting.md`
- `docs/reference/output-formats.md` if verify/repair/clean JSON examples change
- `docs/reference/README.md` and any other command-reference pages that mention removed flags or old terminology
- `ai-resources/skills/ai-resource-manager/SKILL.md`
- `ai-resources/skills/ai-resource-manager/references/install-uninstall.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- Any other docs/help references discovered during implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Current docs describe owned-folder cleanup, manifest reconciliation, and deprecated `verify --fix` behavior accurately
- [ ] Removed flags are absent from active examples, command references, and troubleshooting guidance
- [ ] Migration guidance covers `clean && repair` and the reinstall-on-repair interaction with uninstall/manual removal
- [ ] A migration/upgrade note maps old usage to new equivalents: `repair --reset` → `repair`, `repair --force` → `repair`, `clean --yes` → `clean`, and `repair --reset --force` → `clean && repair`
- [ ] AI resource manager skill content is aligned with the new command semantics
- [ ] Breaking behavior changes and any structured-output changes are called out in release-facing docs or changelog entries
close_reason: Updated docs and ai-resource-manager guidance for owned-folder clean/repair semantics, migration mapping, and repair/clean output model

title: Prepare Release Commit for v3.3.0
description:
## Description
This project does not have version files. Prepare the release commit by committing and pushing the changelog update only.

## Instructions
1. Confirm the rule in `docs/contributor-guide/release-process.md`: version is managed by Git tags only; do not edit `pkg/version/version.go` or any other version placeholder file.
2. Stage the release documentation changes from the previous task (normally `CHANGELOG.md`, plus any docs that actually required correction).
3. Create a conventional commit with `git commit -m "docs: update CHANGELOG for v3.3.0"`.
4. Rebase onto the latest remote main with `git pull --rebase origin main`.
5. Push the commit to `origin/main`.
6. Verify `git status --short --branch` is clean and that `main` is aligned with `origin/main` before moving to tag creation.

## Files to Inspect
- CHANGELOG.md — stage and commit the prepared release entry
- pkg/version/version.go — inspect only; do not edit because release version is injected by ldflags
- .goreleaser.yaml — inspect only if version-injection behavior needs confirmation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No manual version files were changed
- [ ] A release-prep commit exists on `main` with the changelog update
- [ ] The commit is rebased onto the latest `origin/main`
- [ ] The commit is pushed to `origin/main`
- [ ] The working tree is clean after the push
- [ ] The repository is ready for annotated tag creation
close_reason: Release changelog commit pushed to main

title: Create GitHub Release v3.3.0
description:
## Description
Publish `v3.3.0` by pushing an annotated git tag. This repository uses tag-triggered GitHub Actions in `.github/workflows/release.yml`; do not start the workflow manually.

## Instructions
1. Confirm `main` is clean and already pushed.
2. Create the annotated tag with `git tag -a v3.3.0 -m "Release v3.3.0"`.
3. Push the tag with `git push origin v3.3.0`.
4. Identify the tag-triggered workflow run with `gh run list --workflow release.yml --limit 5`.
5. Watch the matching release run until completion and stop immediately if it fails.
6. If the release workflow fails, follow the rollback procedure from `docs/contributor-guide/release-process.md`: delete the GitHub release, delete the local tag, delete the remote tag, and file a follow-up bug instead of retrying blindly.
7. Verify the GitHub release exists with `gh release view v3.3.0`.

## Files to Inspect
- .github/workflows/release.yml — confirms tag-triggered release via GoReleaser
- .goreleaser.yaml — expected release artifacts
- docs/contributor-guide/release-process.md — rollback procedure if publishing fails

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Annotated tag `v3.3.0` exists locally and remotely
- [ ] The release workflow triggered from the tag completes successfully
- [ ] GitHub release `v3.3.0` is published
- [ ] Rollback steps are followed if the workflow fails
- [ ] No manual `gh workflow run` step was used
close_reason: Annotated tag pushed and GitHub release published

title: Acceptance Review: Release v3.3.0
description:
## Description
Perform the final release acceptance review after the tag, release artifacts, and curated notes are published.

## Owner
verifier

## Instructions
1. Verify the release metadata with `gh release view v3.3.0 --json tagName,isDraft,isImmutable,url,assets`.
2. Confirm the required GoReleaser assets exist for linux, darwin, and windows on `amd64` and `arm64`, plus `checksums.txt`, based on `.goreleaser.yaml`.
3. Download one shipped binary archive such as `aimgr_3.3.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz`, extract it, and run `./aimgr --version` to confirm it reports `3.3.0`.
4. Download `checksums.txt` from the release and verify the downloaded archive with `sha256sum -c checksums.txt --ignore-missing`.
5. Verify installation from the published module tag using an isolated bin dir: `TMPBIN=$(mktemp -d) && GOBIN="$TMPBIN" go install github.com/dynatrace-oss/ai-config-manager/v3/cmd/aimgr@v3.3.0 && "$TMPBIN/aimgr" --version`.
6. If any verification fails, file follow-up bugs/tasks instead of reopening closed work.

## Files to Inspect

- [ ] The downloaded archive passes checksum verification against `checksums.txt`
- [ ] `go install ...@v3.3.0` succeeds in an isolated temp bin
- [ ] Any new failures are tracked as new beads issues
