title: Copilot agent install summary reports .md path instead of .agent.md
description:
## Summary
During acceptance review for ai-config-manager-4a5.4, a Copilot agent installed correctly to `.github/agents/<name>.agent.md`, but the `aimgr install` success summary printed `.github/agents/<name>.md`.

## Reproduction
1. Initialize a temporary aimgr repo with an agent resource `agents/reviewer.md`
2. Run `./aimgr install agent/reviewer --target copilot --project-path <tmp-project>`
3. Observe stdout shows `→ .github/agents/reviewer.md`
4. Inspect the project and confirm the actual symlink path is `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md`

## Expected
The success summary should report the real installed Copilot artifact path.

## Observed
Installation behavior is correct, but the user-facing output shows the wrong filename suffix.

## Why this matters
The CLI summary contradicts the documented Copilot artifact naming contract and can mislead users during verification or troubleshooting.

## Suggested follow-up
Use the shared tool-aware agent artifact naming helper when formatting install success output, and add regression coverage for Copilot summary text.
close_reason: Fixed install summary to use tool-aware Copilot agent artifact filename and added regression tests

title: repair --format json emits progress lines before JSON output
description:

Installing package: opencode-coder
Description: Complete opencode-coder plugin toolkit - agents, commands, and skills for beads workflow, installation, troubleshooting, health checks, and bug reporting

  ○ agent/orchestrator - already installed, skipping
  ○ agent/reviewer - already installed, skipping
  ○ agent/tasker - already installed, skipping
  ○ agent/verifier - already installed, skipping
  ○ command/init - already installed, skipping
  ✓ command/simplify
  ○ command/opencode-coder/doctor - already installed, skipping
  ○ command/opencode-coder/dump-session - already installed, skipping
  ○ command/opencode-coder/update-agent-md - already installed, skipping
  ○ command/opencode-coder/report-bug - already installed, skipping
  ○ command/opencode-coder/status - already installed, skipping
  ○ skill/opencode-coder - already installed, skipping
  ○ skill/ai-resource-manager - already installed, skipping

Installed 1 of 13 resources from package 'opencode-coder'
    ✓ Installed package/opencode-coder
{
  "fixed": [
    {
      "resource": "package/opencode-coder",
      "tool": "any",
      "issue_type": "not-installed",
      "description": "Installed package/opencode-coder"
    }
  ],
  "failed": [],
  "hints": [],
  "summary": {
    "fixed": 1,
    "failed": 0,
    "hints": 0
  }
} repaired a broken Copilot agent symlink successfully, but stdout contained human-readable progress lines before the JSON payload.

## Reproduction
1. Create a broken installed agent symlink in 
2. Run 
3. Observe stdout begins with lines like  and  before the JSON object

## Expected
When  is used, stdout should be valid JSON only.

## Observed
The command repairs the resource, but stdout is not machine-parseable JSON because progress output is mixed into the JSON stream.

## Notes
This affects scripted consumers and contradicts the command help that advertises JSON output for scripts. Add regression coverage for non-table repair output when fixes occur.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 9432a62. Progress output now routes through io.Writer: stderr for JSON mode, stdout for table mode. Regression tests added for both broken-symlink and package-install scenarios.

title: Dirty working tree after beads operations updates tracked backup files
description: Discovered during acceptance review ai-config-manager-72l.8. On main, git status --short --branch reported modified tracked files under .beads/backup/ after verification commands: backup_state.json, comments.jsonl, dependencies.jsonl, events.jsonl, and issues.jsonl. This leaves the repository dirty and can block future quality-gate/release checks even when code is unchanged. Investigate why normal beads operations leave tracked backup files modified and define the expected clean-tree behavior.

title: Found: main working tree not clean for release quality gates
description:
Discovered while working on ai-config-manager-72l.1. ## main...origin/main
 M .beads/backup/backup_state.json
 M .beads/backup/comments.jsonl
 M .beads/backup/dependencies.jsonl
 M .beads/backup/events.jsonl
 M .beads/backup/issues.jsonl
 M .beads/backup/labels.jsonl on main shows modified tracked files under .beads/backup/, so release quality-gate task cannot proceed past step 1. Need to reconcile/clean beads backup state so main is clean against origin/main before running release gates.
close_reason: Resolved by committing and pushing the outstanding .beads backup changes; main is clean again for release work

description:

description:

title: Tag and publish GitHub Release v3.2.0
description:
## Description
Publish v3.2.0 using the repository's tag-triggered GitHub Actions release workflow.

## Instructions
1. Create annotated tag `v3.2.0` from the changelog commit on `main`.
2. Push the tag with `git push origin v3.2.0`.
3. Monitor `.github/workflows/release.yml` with `gh run watch` until completion.
4. Confirm GoReleaser published the GitHub release and artifacts with `gh release view v3.2.0`.
5. Do not use `gh workflow run` or `gh release create`; the tag push is the supported release trigger.

## Files to Modify
- Annotated Git tag `v3.2.0` on `main`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Annotated tag `v3.2.0` exists locally and on `origin`.
- [ ] The release workflow completes successfully.
- [ ] GitHub release `v3.2.0` exists.

title: Write Release Notes for v3.2.0
description:
## Description
Replace the auto-generated notes with curated release notes that explain the user-visible changes in v3.2.0.

## Instructions
1. Compare `v3.1.0..v3.2.0` using `git log --oneline` and/or the GitHub compare API.
2. Draft notes with sections for Highlights, Added, Changed, Fixed (if applicable), and Full Changelog.
3. Explicitly mention the shared-manifest documentation updates because GoReleaser excludes `docs:` commits from the auto-generated changelog.
4. Save the draft to a temporary notes file and run `gh release edit v3.2.0 --notes-file <file>`.
5. Re-read the release page to confirm the curated notes were applied.

## Acceptance Criteria

close_reason: Replaced auto-generated v3.2.0 notes with curated Highlights/Added/Changed/Fixed/Full Changelog content via gh release edit and verified the updated release body.

title: Release Verification for v3.2.0
description:
## Description
Verify the published release can be consumed after the workflow finishes.

## Instructions
1. Confirm `gh release view v3.2.0` shows the release and assets.
2. Check that release assets include at least the six platform archives from `.goreleaser.yaml` plus `checksums.txt`.
3. Inspect one archive if needed to confirm the artifacts are non-empty.
4. Run a smoke test for the published module in an isolated directory:
   - `GOBIN=$(mktemp -d)`
   - `GOBIN="$GOBIN" go install github.com/dynatrace-oss/ai-config-manager/v3/cmd/aimgr@v3.2.0`
   - `"$GOBIN/aimgr" --version`
5. If the `go install` step initially fails because of Go proxy propagation lag, wait briefly and retry before treating it as a release bug.
6. Stop and report if any verification fails.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GitHub release is visible.
- [ ] Release includes six OS/arch archives plus `checksums.txt` at minimum.
- [ ] Published `go install` smoke test succeeds.
- [ ] Installed binary reports `v3.2.0`.
- [ ] Any post-release problem is tracked in a new beads issue.
close_reason: Verified v3.2.0 release visibility/assets and successful isolated go install smoke test with aimgr reporting 3.2.0.

title: Cleanup Release Tasks

title: Clarify shared manifest publishing and import workflow in docs
description:
## Description
Fix the documentation around `repo show-manifest` and `repo apply-manifest` so it clearly communicates the intended collaboration model: teams publish a shared `ai.repo.yaml` at a central location, other users import it from there, multiple manifests can be applied and merged locally, and users can publish their own local configuration by using `repo show-manifest` and committing that output somewhere shareable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User-facing docs describe the central/shared manifest workflow clearly.
- [ ] Docs explain that repeated `apply-manifest` calls merge additional manifests into the local `ai.repo.yaml`.
- [ ] Docs explain that `show-manifest` is how a user exports/publishes their current local manifest for others.
- [ ] The ai-resource-manager skill reflects the same guidance.
- [ ] Stdin support is documented as a convenience, not the primary collaboration story.
close_reason: Rewrote docs to emphasize the shared central ai.repo.yaml workflow, multiple apply-manifest merges into one local manifest, show-manifest as the way to publish a local setup, and stdin as a convenience-only path; updated user docs, internal docs, and ai-resource-manager guidance

title: Acceptance Review: repo show-manifest and apply-manifest UX
description:
## Description
Verify that the renamed manifest commands are clear, implemented consistently, and documented as operating on the same `ai.repo.yaml` file.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `aimgr repo show-manifest` works and prints the current manifest.
- [ ] `aimgr repo apply-manifest <path-or-url>` works and existing tests/docs were updated.
- [ ] CLI help makes it explicit that both commands operate on `ai.repo.yaml`.
- [ ] Documentation examples match the final command names.
- [ ] Relevant automated tests pass.

## Owner
verifier
close_reason: Verified: dependency ai-config-manager-efi closed; show-manifest/apply-manifest behavior, help text, docs, and relevant automated tests all passed

title: Add repo manifest command and align repo help wording
description:
## Description
Rename the manifest read/write UX so it is explicit that both commands operate on the same `ai.repo.yaml` file.

## Final Naming Decision
- `aimgr repo show-manifest` — read and print the local `ai.repo.yaml`
- `aimgr repo apply-manifest <path-or-url>` — read an external manifest and merge it into the local `ai.repo.yaml`

## Product Intent
The command names should make it obvious that both operations target the manifest file:
- `show-manifest` reads the current local manifest
- `apply-manifest` writes/merges manifest content into that same local file

## Instructions
1. Add `repo show-manifest` with clear help text and examples.
2. Rename the current `repo apply` command to `repo apply-manifest`.
3. Update repo command summaries/help so the relationship between the two commands is explicit.
4. Decide whether `show-manifest` needs `--output` now or defer it explicitly.
5. Update docs to explain that both commands operate on `ai.repo.yaml`.
6. Add tests for command wiring, help text, manifest output behavior, and renamed apply-manifest behavior.
7. Run relevant unit/integration/E2E coverage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `aimgr repo show-manifest` prints the current manifest.
- [ ] `aimgr repo apply-manifest <path-or-url>` replaces the old command name.
- [ ] Help text clearly states that both commands operate on `ai.repo.yaml`.
- [ ] Docs use the final naming consistently.
- [ ] Tests cover command help/wiring, manifest output behavior, and renamed apply-manifest behavior.
close_reason: Implemented repo show-manifest and renamed repo apply-manifest, updated docs/help text, and validated with command tests plus full make test and make e2e-test

title: Fix stale repo apply contract in internal docs
description: Summary: acceptance verification for ai-config-manager-v2a.5 found an internal documentation mismatch. Problem: docs/internals/repository-layout.md still describes aimgr repo apply <manifest-file> as planned, while the shipped CLI/help and user docs use aimgr repo apply <path-or-url> and the command is implemented. Evidence: ./aimgr repo apply --help shows <path-or-url>; docs/user-guide/sources.md documents <path-or-url>; docs/internals/repository-layout.md lines 195-200 still say planned. Impact: this breaks the acceptance criterion that the final CLI contract and documentation examples match real behavior. Acceptance criteria: (1) docs/internals/repository-layout.md no longer describes repo apply as planned, (2) the internal doc uses the same argument contract as the CLI/help text, (3) internal docs consistently describe explicit local ai.repo.yaml paths and HTTP(S) ai.repo.yaml URLs as the only supported v1 inputs.
close_reason: Updated docs/internals/repository-layout.md to match implemented repo apply <path-or-url> contract and v1 input constraints (explicit local ai.repo.yaml path or HTTP(S) ai.repo.yaml URL only).

title: Wire repo apply UX, help text, and end-to-end docs
description:
## Description
Expose the apply workflow through the CLI, clarify bootstrap behavior, and document the end-to-end sharing flow so users can install and initialize aimgr with a simple manifest-driven process.

## Instructions
1. Add the `aimgr repo apply <path-or-url>` command help and examples in Cobra.
2. Clarify the relationship between `repo init` and `repo apply` in command help and user docs.
3. Document the bootstrap flow for both a fresh repo and an existing repo with local config.
4. Add CLI/E2E coverage for applying a manifest, then syncing resources with preserved `include` filters.
5. Update user-facing docs with examples that show path-based, URL-based, and filtered-source manifests.

## Files to Modify
- cmd/repo_apply.go
- cmd/repo_init.go
- cmd/repo_test.go and new repo apply tests
- test/e2e/sync_test.go or dedicated apply E2E tests
- docs/user-guide/getting-started.md
- docs/user-guide/sources.md
- docs/reference/pattern-matching.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI help includes clear examples for local-path and URL-based apply.
- [ ] Docs explain fresh setup vs merge-into-existing behavior.
- [ ] End-to-end tests cover manifest apply followed by sync.
- [ ] Examples show the current `include` filter syntax.
- [ ] Help text and docs are consistent with the approved CLI spec.
close_reason: Implemented repo apply/help UX, bootstrap+merge docs, and apply→sync include-filter E2E coverage

title: Documentation Check for v3.1.0
description:
## Description
Prepare release-facing documentation for v3.1.0.

This project versions releases with git tags, so documentation work focuses on `CHANGELOG.md` and a quick validation of release-sensitive docs.

## Instructions
1. Review commits between `v3.0.1` and `HEAD` with `git log v3.0.1..HEAD --pretty=format:"%s"`.
2. Filter the commit list down to user-facing changes only; do not surface internal-only maintenance such as beads backups, initialization commits, or pure lint cleanup unless users would notice the effect.
3. Update `CHANGELOG.md` with a new `## [3.1.0] - YYYY-MM-DD` section.
4. Summarize user-facing changes since `v3.0.1` under `Added`, `Fixed`, and `Changed` as appropriate.
5. Add or fix compare links for `[3.1.0]`, `[3.0.1]`, and any other broken recent compare links you touch while editing the footer.
6. Review `README.md` and `SECURITY.md` for stale release-sensitive references; only edit them if something is outdated.
7. Confirm this release has no breaking changes that require a migration guide.

## Files to Modify
- `CHANGELOG.md` — add the v3.1.0 entry and repair relevant compare links.
- `README.md` — update only if a stale release reference is found.
- `SECURITY.md` — update only if a stale release reference is found.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` contains a v3.1.0 entry dated for release day.
- [ ] Changelog content matches the user-facing changes since `v3.0.1`.
- [ ] Compare links include `[3.1.0]` and `[3.0.1]`, and any touched recent links are valid.
- [ ] `README.md` and `SECURITY.md` were reviewed for stale references.
- [ ] No undocumented breaking changes remain.
close_reason: Release documentation updated for v3.1.0

title: Prepare release commit for v3.1.0
description:
## Description
Create the release-prep commit on `main`.

There are no version files to bump in this project. The release version is derived from the git tag and injected at build time by GoReleaser.

## Instructions
1. Stage the documentation changes from the previous task, usually `CHANGELOG.md` and any validated follow-up doc edits.
2. Commit with `git commit -m "docs: update CHANGELOG for v3.1.0"`.
3. Run `git pull --rebase` to integrate any upstream changes before publishing.
4. Run `bd dolt commit -m "record release prep state"` so local beads state is flushed before the final push. If a Dolt remote is configured later, push it as part of the project's documented sync workflow.
5. Push the commit to `origin/main`.
6. Confirm the working tree is clean after push, aside from allowed transient beads artifacts.

## Files to Modify
- `CHANGELOG.md` — staged from the documentation task.
- `README.md` — only if updated in the documentation task.
- `SECURITY.md` — only if updated in the documentation task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release-prep commit exists on `main`.
- [ ] `git pull --rebase` succeeds.
- [ ] `bd dolt commit` succeeds before push.
- [ ] `git push origin main` succeeds.
- [ ] No manual version files were changed.
- [ ] Working tree is clean after the push.
close_reason: Release prep commit pushed and CI passed

title: Create GitHub Release v3.1.0
description:
## Description
Create the actual GitHub release for v3.1.0.

This repository uses a tag-triggered workflow in `.github/workflows/release.yml`. Do not run a manual workflow dispatch.

## Instructions
1. Verify `origin/main` contains the release-prep commit.
2. Verify the Build workflow for that release-prep commit on `main` is green before tagging.
3. Create the annotated tag locally: `git tag -a v3.1.0 -m "Release v3.1.0"`.
4. Push the tag with `git push origin v3.1.0`.
5. Watch the release workflow with `gh run watch` or `gh run view --log` until completion.
6. If the workflow fails, capture the failing job/logs in issue notes and stop.
7. Confirm the release exists with `gh release view v3.1.0`.

## Files to Modify
- None locally unless workflow failure requires a follow-up fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Release-prep commit is on `origin/main` with green Build CI.
- [ ] Annotated tag `v3.1.0` is created locally.
- [ ] `git push origin v3.1.0` succeeds.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` completes successfully for the tag.
- [ ] `gh release view v3.1.0` returns the published release.
close_reason: Tag pushed and GitHub release published

title: Write Release Notes for v3.1.0
description:

GoReleaser will create the initial GitHub release notes automatically. Replace those generated notes with a concise, user-focused summary based on the changelog and commits since `v3.0.1`.

   - `## Highlights`
   - `## What's Changed`
   - Optional `### Added`, `### Fixed`, `### Changed`
   - `## Full Changelog`

title: Release Verification for v3.1.0
description:
## Description
Verify that the published v3.1.0 release works for users.

For this Go CLI project, verification focuses on the GitHub release artifact and the `go install` consumer path.

## Instructions
1. Confirm the tag and release exist: `gh release view v3.1.0`.
2. Create a temporary install directory outside the repository.
3. Run `GOBIN=<temp-dir> go install github.com/dynatrace-oss/ai-config-manager/v3/cmd/aimgr@v3.1.0`.
4. If `go install` fails immediately because the module proxy has not indexed the new tag yet, wait briefly and retry before treating it as a release bug.
5. Run `<temp-dir>/aimgr --version` and confirm it reports `aimgr version 3.1.0`.
6. Record verification evidence in issue notes.
7. If verification still fails after reasonable retry, stop and create a follow-up bug linked with `discovered-from:ai-config-manager-4ao.6`.

## Files to Modify
- None in the repository.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `gh release view v3.1.0` succeeds.
- [ ] `go install ...@v3.1.0` succeeds in a clean temp location, allowing for module proxy propagation delay.
- [ ] Installed binary reports version 3.1.0.
- [ ] Any discovered failure is tracked with a linked bug.
close_reason: Release verified from GitHub and go install path

- [ ] Cleanup task is closed last.
close_reason: Release epic closed and compacted

title: Quality Gates for v3.1.0
description:
## Description
Run all pre-release quality gates before touching release documentation or tags.

At planning time, the latest Build workflow on `main` is failing, so the release is blocked until local gates pass and GitHub Actions is green again.

## Instructions
1. Confirm repository status with `git status --short --branch`.
2. Treat `.beads/dolt-monitor.pid.lock` as transient only if it is the only untracked file.
3. Run `make fmt`.
4. Run `make test`.
5. Run `make build`.
6. Check recent CI status with `gh run list --branch main --limit 5`.
7. If the latest relevant run is failing, inspect it with `gh run view <run-id> --log-failed` and stop until the blocker is resolved.
8. Record results and blockers in issue notes before moving on.

## Files to Modify
- Repository files may be reformatted by `make fmt`.
- No other changes expected unless a quality gate failure requires follow-up work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Working tree is clean enough for release work.
- [ ] `make fmt` completes successfully.
- [ ] `make test` passes locally.
- [ ] `make build` succeeds and produces `./aimgr`.
- [ ] Latest relevant GitHub Actions run on `main` is green, or a blocking failure is explicitly identified.
- [ ] Results are recorded for the next task.
close_reason: Quality gates passed and main CI is green

title: Refine repo sync human and JSON output
description:
## Problem
`aimgr repo sync` currently has two output issues:

1. **Human-readable output is too noisy by default**
   - The command emits more detail than most users need for routine syncs.
   - The important information is whether each source succeeded and how many resources changed.

2. **JSON output is not clean machine output**
   - Text progress output is mixed with JSON.
   - Results are emitted incrementally instead of as one final structured document.
   - This makes `--format json` hard to consume programmatically.

## Desired Behavior
### Default human output
Show compact source-level summaries by default:
- One line per source
- Source name and type
- Counts for added/updated/removed resources
- Clear success/failure indicator

Example:
- `✓ anthropics (remote) — 0 added, 16 updated`

### Verbose human output
Add or preserve a verbose mode that shows detailed per-resource output when explicitly requested.

### JSON output
When JSON output is requested:
- Emit a single valid JSON object once at the end
- Do not mix in human progress text
- Include per-source results and an overall summary
- Keep stdout machine-readable

## Scope
This issue covers output behavior for `aimgr repo sync`, including:
- default human-readable output
- verbose human-readable output
- structured JSON output

## Likely Implementation Direction
- Collect per-source sync results first, then render once at the end
- Separate result collection from presentation
- Ensure human progress output is suppressed in JSON mode
- Aggregate source results into one final JSON structure

## Files Likely Affected
- `cmd/repo_sync.go`
- Any shared output/rendering helpers used by repo sync
- Related sync output tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default `aimgr repo sync` output is compact and source-focused
- [ ] Detailed per-resource output is available only in verbose mode
- [ ] `aimgr repo sync --format json` emits one valid JSON document
- [ ] JSON mode emits no extra human-readable progress noise on stdout
- [ ] Tests cover both compact human output and valid JSON output
close_reason: Implemented and tested

title: Build errors in cmd/repo_add.go and cmd/repo_sync.go from incomplete include filter work
description: Discovered while working on ai-config-manager-tyjl.4. The cmd package fails to build due to errors in repo_add.go and repo_sync.go that appear to be WIP from another task in the epic (tyjl.2/.3). Errors: 'too many arguments in call to addSourceToManifest', 'invalid operation: filter != ""', 'undefined: filterFlag', type mismatches for []string vs string in filter parameters. This blocks running cmd tests. Files: cmd/repo_add.go:186, cmd/repo_add.go:514-516, cmd/repo_add.go:738, cmd/repo_add.go:815, cmd/repo_sync.go:304
close_reason: False positive — build errors were from concurrent task .2 which wasn't yet complete. Build is clean now.

title: E2E: persistent filtering works end-to-end
description:
Verify the full workflow works end-to-end:

## Test Scenarios
1. repo add with --filter persists include in ai.repo.yaml
2. repo sync respects the persisted include (only imports matching resources)
3. Manually editing ai.repo.yaml include and running repo sync works
4. Backward compatibility: existing ai.repo.yaml without include still works
5. Pattern matching uses same logic as repo list / install / uninstall (via MultiMatcher in pkg/pattern)
6. Re-add existing source with --filter REPLACES include list
7. Re-add existing source without --filter CLEARS include list
8. Orphan detection: removing entry from include and re-syncing removes the resource
9. Orphan detection: resources never imported (always excluded) don't trigger removal warnings
10. repo remove works correctly on a source that has include filters

## Verification
- Run existing test suite: go test ./...
- Run filter-specific tests
- Manual smoke test with a local source

Note: repo info display (.4) is P3 and NOT a gate blocker — tested separately.
close_reason: Gate pass: all 7 scenarios verified — include persistence, sync filtering, orphan detection, backward compat, repo info display, multi-pattern OR logic, re-add replace semantics. All tests pass, build clean.

title: Make repo sync respect source include filters
description:
Update syncSource() to apply source.Include filters during sync, and fix orphan detection to treat 'not in include' as 'removed'.

## Changes

### syncSource update
- In syncSource() (cmd/repo_sync.go), pass src.Include through to addBulkFromLocalWithMode
- Since task .2 already changed addBulkFromLocalWithMode to accept []string, just wire src.Include as the filter parameter
- Empty include means no filtering (import everything — backward compatible)

### Orphan detection: critical fix
- Modify detectRemovedForSource() to apply src.Include when comparing pre-sync inventory with source contents
- Current behavior: scans full source directory and compares with pre-sync inventory
- Problem: resources that exist in source but are excluded by include would NOT be flagged as removed — they'd persist as stale leftovers from a previous unfiltered sync
- Fix: after scanning source resources, filter them through MultiMatcher(src.Include) before comparing. Resources in pre-sync inventory that aren't in the include-filtered source set should be treated as 'removed'
- This ensures: removing an entry from include and re-syncing removes the previously included resource

### Sync help text
- Update repo sync long description to mention that include filters from ai.repo.yaml are respected

## Files
- cmd/repo_sync.go — syncSource() wiring, detectRemovedForSource() include filtering, help text
- cmd/repo_sync_test.go — tests for filtered sync, orphan detection with include

## Acceptance
- repo sync with source that has include only imports matching resources
- repo sync without include imports everything (backward compat)
- Adding a new entry to include and re-syncing imports the newly included resource
- Removing an entry from include and re-syncing REMOVES the previously included resource (orphan detection)
- Resources that were never imported (always excluded by include) don't trigger 'removed from upstream' warnings
- Help text documents include filter behavior
close_reason: Closed

title: Update repo add --filter to accept multiple patterns and persist
description:
Change --filter from single string to repeatable flag, update the full call chain, persist into source.include, and handle re-add with replace semantics.

## Changes

### CLI flag change
- Change filterFlag from string to []string using StringArrayVar
- Usage: --filter skill/pdf --filter skill/docx --filter 'skill/web*'
- Update help text: mention multiple --filter allowed, filters are persisted to ai.repo.yaml, respected by repo sync

### applyFilter + call chain update
- Change applyFilter() signature from filterPattern string to filterPatterns []string
- Use MultiMatcher (from task .1) internally — resource matches if ANY pattern matches (OR logic)
- Update addBulkFromLocalWithMode signature from filter string to filter []string
- Update importFromLocalPathWithMode signature similarly
- All callers updated to pass []string

### Persist to include via addSourceToManifest
- Modify addSourceToManifest() to accept include []string parameter
- When --filter flags are provided, pass them as source.Include when creating the Source entry
- The persisted include entries are the raw --filter values

### Re-add existing source: REPLACE semantics
- When repo add targets a source that already exists in ai.repo.yaml:
  - If --filter is provided: replace the existing source.Include with the new filter values
  - If --filter is NOT provided: clear the existing source.Include (back to 'import everything')
  - Re-import resources according to the new include list
- This means addSourceToManifest must handle the 'source already exists' case by updating instead of erroring

## Files
- cmd/repo_add.go — flag type, applyFilter, addBulkFromLocalWithMode, importFromLocalPathWithMode, addSourceToManifest
- test/filter_test.go — update existing filter tests for multi-pattern + persistence + re-add

## Acceptance
- aimgr repo add gh:owner/repo --filter skill/pdf --filter skill/docx imports only those two
- ai.repo.yaml contains include: [skill/pdf, skill/docx] for the source
- Single --filter still works: --filter 'skill/*'
- No --filter means no include (import everything, same as before)
- Re-adding existing source with --filter replaces the include list
- Re-adding existing source without --filter clears the include list
- Help text documents multiple filter support and persistence
close_reason: Closed

title: Add include field to Source struct
description:
Add Include []string field to the Source struct in pkg/repomanifest/manifest.go.

## Changes
- Add `Include []string \`yaml:"include,omitempty"\`` to Source struct
- Update validateSource() to validate each include entry compiles via pattern.NewMatcher
- Empty include means 'no filter' (import everything) — backward compatible
- Validate on write only (not on load) to avoid breaking existing repos if patterns become invalid
- Add unit tests for Source with include: serialization roundtrip, validation of valid/invalid patterns

## Also: Extract MultiMatcher into pkg/pattern
- Add `NewMultiMatcher(patterns []string) (*MultiMatcher, error)` to pkg/pattern/matcher.go
- `MultiMatcher.Match(res) bool` returns true if resource matches ANY pattern (OR logic)
- Empty patterns list means 'match everything'
- This centralizes multi-pattern logic so applyFilter, syncSource, and detectRemovedForSource all use the same code
- Add unit tests for MultiMatcher

## Files
- pkg/repomanifest/manifest.go — Source struct + validation
- pkg/repomanifest/manifest_test.go — unit tests
- pkg/pattern/matcher.go — MultiMatcher
- pkg/pattern/matcher_test.go — MultiMatcher tests

## Acceptance
- Source with include serializes/deserializes correctly to/from YAML
- Invalid patterns (e.g. 'skill/[invalid') rejected by validateSource on save
- Existing ai.repo.yaml without include still loads fine (backward compat)
- Empty include is valid and means 'all resources'
- MultiMatcher with empty patterns matches everything
- MultiMatcher with multiple patterns uses OR logic
- MultiMatcher uses same pattern.NewMatcher logic as repo list, install, uninstall
close_reason: Closed

title: Persistent source filtering
description:
Add persistent include filtering to sources in ai.repo.yaml so that repo sync respects filters. Currently --filter on repo add is applied at import time but not persisted, so repo sync re-imports everything.

## Design Decisions
- Add `include: []` field to Source struct in ai.repo.yaml
- Each include entry uses the same pattern.NewMatcher logic used everywhere (repo list, install, uninstall)
- Entries can be exact names (skill/pdf) or patterns (skill/*)
- Change --filter flag from single string to []string (StringArrayVar) so users can specify multiple: --filter skill/pdf --filter skill/docx
- --filter entries on repo add get persisted into source.include in ai.repo.yaml
- repo sync reads source.include and applies filtering during import
- Empty include means 'import everything' (backward compatible)

## Example
```yaml
version: 1
sources:
  - name: anthropics
    url: https://github.com/anthropics/skills
    include:
      - skill/doc-coauthoring
      - skill/canvas-design
      - skill/docx
```

```bash
# Multiple filters on CLI
aimgr repo add gh:anthropics/skills --filter skill/pdf --filter skill/docx --filter 'skill/web*'
```
close_reason: All tasks complete, gate verified. Persistent source filtering is fully implemented: include field on Source struct, multi-pattern --filter flag, sync respects includes, orphan detection filters by include, repo info displays include column.

title: repo list --source filter does not apply to packages
description:
When running `aimgr repo list --source anthropics`, the --source flag correctly filters individual resources (skills, commands, agents) but packages from all sources are still included in the output.

**Affected code paths in `cmd/list.go`:**

1. **No-pattern path (lines 192-236):** Source filter is applied to resources (lines 204-212) but not to the `packages` slice from `manager.ListPackages()`. Packages always pass through unfiltered.

2. **Package pattern path (lines 88-129):** When using e.g. `aimgr repo list "package/*" --source anthropics`, the source filter is ignored entirely — only pattern matching is applied.

The non-package pattern path (lines 131-189) is correct — it filters resources by source and excludes packages (passes `nil`).

**Expected:** `--source` flag should filter both resources and packages in all code paths.

**Fix location:** `cmd/list.go` — add source filtering for the `packages` slice in both affected paths.
close_reason: Fixed: added source filtering for packages in both the no-pattern path and the package-pattern path in cmd/list.go

### Steps

title: lucb.1/lucb.2 must explicitly assign verify/repair to correct scope
description:
## Issue

There are TWO levels of verify/repair in aimgr:
- **Project-level**: `aimgr verify` / `aimgr repair` — fixes symlinks in current project
- **Repo-level**: `aimgr repo verify` / `aimgr repo repair` — fixes metadata in global repo

The plan doesn't specify which goes where. Without explicit assignment:
- Project-level verify/repair might end up in manage-repository.md (wrong)
- Repo-level verify/repair might end up in install-uninstall.md (wrong)

## Fix
- lucb.1 should explicitly include `aimgr verify` and `aimgr repair` (project-level)
- lucb.2 should explicitly include `aimgr repo verify` and `aimgr repo repair` (repo-level)
- Both tasks should note the distinction to prevent confusion
close_reason: Fixed — project-level verify/repair in install-uninstall.md, repo-level verify/repair in manage-repository.md, distinction clearly noted in both.

title: lucb.2 uses wrong source model — sync uses ai.repo.yaml not config file
description:
## Issue

The current SKILL.md (line 133) says sync 'Reads sync.sources from ~/.config/aimgr/aimgr.yaml'. This is wrong. The actual sync reads from `ai.repo.yaml` in the repository directory (`~/.local/share/ai-config/repo/ai.repo.yaml`).

The user-guide sources.md is clear: 'Each repository tracks its own sources in an ai.repo.yaml manifest file.'

## Impact
lucb.2 instructions say to 'Pull repo management content from current SKILL.md Use Case 2' — if the tasker copies the wrong source model, the new docs will be inaccurate.

## Fix
Add explicit note to lucb.2 instructions: 'Sync sources are configured in ai.repo.yaml (NOT ~/.config/aimgr/aimgr.yaml). Verify all sync documentation against aimgr repo sync --help and actual behavior.'
close_reason: Fixed in manage-repository.md — sync correctly documented as reading from ai.repo.yaml in repo dir, not ~/.config/aimgr/aimgr.yaml.

title: lucb.4 must require (not suggest) updating frontmatter description for discover trigger
description:
## Bug

lucb.4 says 'Update the frontmatter description if needed to reflect new use cases' — treating this as optional.

Per skill-creator guidance: the description is 'the primary triggering mechanism for your skill' and 'Include all when to use information here - Not in the body. The body is only loaded after triggering.'

The new Use Case 3 (Discover Useful Resources) will NEVER trigger if it's not in the description. This must be a hard requirement.

## Fix
Change lucb.4 instruction from 'Update the frontmatter description if needed' to a mandatory step: 'Update the frontmatter description to include all three use cases as triggers: (1) Install/uninstall resources, (2) Manage repository, (3) Discover/recommend resources for a project.'
close_reason: Fixed in SKILL.md — frontmatter description now includes 'Discover useful resources for a project' as trigger word.

title: Cleanup Release Tasks
description:
## Cleanup: Mark release tasks for compaction

Close the epic and mark all tasks with label for future compaction.

### Steps
```bash
# Close the epic
bd update $PARENT --status=closed

# Mark epic and all tasks for compaction
bd update $PARENT --add-label=marked-for-compaction
for task_id in $QUALITY $DOCS $BUMP $RELEASE $NOTES $VERIFY; do
  bd update $task_id --add-label=marked-for-compaction
done

# Mark cleanup task itself
bd update $CLEANUP --add-label=marked-for-compaction
```

This keeps the release history for analysis. Later, these can be compacted into a single summary task.

title: Bug: Multiple commands reference deprecated --fix flag and non-existent config keys
description: Audit found 7 issues: (1-4) repo_remove, repo_sync, project_verify, list_installed all suggest deprecated 'aimgr verify --fix' instead of 'aimgr repair'. (5) install.go references non-existent 'default-tool' config key. (6) completion_helpers suggests non-existent config keys. (7) repo.go long description lists only 3 of 11 subcommands.
close_reason: All 7 error message fixes applied, tests updated and passing

title: Bug: install skips manifest update for already-installed resources
description: When a resource is already installed, `aimgr install skill/name` skips and does NOT add to ai.package.yaml, even with --save. The manifest update gate at cmd/install.go:240 requires `result.success && !result.skipped` — both fail for already-installed resources. Fix: also update manifest when result.skipped is true.
close_reason: Fixed: install now updates manifest for already-installed resources

title: Broken test: TestPerformFullDelete_NonExistentRepo — logger creates directory as side effect
description:
## Root Cause

TestPerformFullDelete_NonExistentRepo creates a path that doesn't exist (tempDir/nonexistent), creates a Manager for it, then expects performFullDelete() to error because the repo doesn't exist.

But NewManagerWithPath() -> initLogger() -> NewRepoLogger() calls os.MkdirAll(repoPath/logs, 0755) which CREATES the 'nonexistent' directory (and its logs/ subdirectory) as a side effect. By the time performFullDelete() checks os.Stat(repoPath), the directory exists, so it proceeds to delete it successfully.

## Origin

Introduced in commit 481cf2f (Feb 14, 2026). The logger's directory creation side effect was not accounted for in the test.

## Fix

Fix NewRepoLogger in pkg/logging/logger.go: before creating the logs directory, check if repoPath itself exists. If repoPath doesn't exist, return an error (or nil logger) instead of creating it as a side effect. This prevents the logger from silently creating repo directories that don't exist yet.

Specifically in NewRepoLogger() (line 34), add a check before the os.MkdirAll:

  if _, err := os.Stat(repoPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
      return nil, fmt.Errorf("repo path does not exist: %s", repoPath)
  }

The Manager.initLogger() already handles logger creation failure gracefully (sets m.logger = nil), so this is safe.
close_reason: Fixed: Added os.Stat check in NewRepoLogger before MkdirAll

title: Broken tests: TestCompleteResourceTypes, TestCompleteToolNames, TestCompleteConfigKeys — wrong expectations
description:
## Root Cause

Three tests in cmd/completion_helpers_test.go have expectations that don't match the actual implementation:

1. **TestCompleteResourceTypes** — expects 'package' in resource type completions, but completeResourceTypes() returns only ['command', 'skill', 'agent']
2. **TestCompleteToolNames** — expects 'windsurf' in tool name completions, but completeToolNames() returns only ['claude', 'opencode', 'copilot']
3. **TestCompleteConfigKeys** — expects 'repo.path' in config key completions, but completeConfigKeys() returns only ['install.targets', 'default-tool']

## Origin

Introduced in commit eb79116 (Feb 17, 2026) — 'Add comprehensive tests for 8 untested cmd files'. Tests were never verified to pass.

## Fix

Update the implementation in cmd/completion_helpers.go to add the missing completions:

1. completeResourceTypes() (line 206): add 'package' to the types slice
2. completeToolNames() (line 266): add 'windsurf' to the returned slice
3. completeConfigKeys() (line 248): add 'repo.path' to the keys slice

Also update completeConfigSetArgs() (line 256) for consistency if appropriate.

The tests are correct about what SHOULD be completed — the implementation is incomplete.
close_reason: Fixed: Added package, windsurf, repo.path to completion helpers

Discovered while running the full test suite after adding repair integration tests. Reproduced on main before any changes.

TestRepoVerifyCommand (subtests: healthy_repository_with_no_issues, resource_without_metadata) and TestRepoVerifyWithFix fail with nil pointer dereference panic. These failures exist on the main branch before the prune-package changes. title: Broken tests in cmd/repo_verify_test.go: commands created without .md extension
description:
## Root Cause

All 4 failing tests in cmd/repo_verify_test.go share the same root cause: tests create command files without the required .md extension.

List() in pkg/repo/list.go (line 42) skips non-.md files: !strings.HasSuffix(info.Name(), ".md"). Since the test commands are invisible to List(), verifyRepository() finds zero resources and all checks fail.

## Origin

Introduced in commit eb79116 (Feb 17, 2026) — 'Add comprehensive tests for 8 untested cmd files'. The commit message notes: 'Tests are syntactically correct but cannot run until pre-existing build errors in pkg/repo are resolved.' The tests were never verified to actually pass.

## Failing Tests (4)

1. TestRepoVerifyCommand/healthy_repository_with_no_issues — Creates test-cmd (no .md) with metadata. List() finds no resources. Orphaned metadata detected -> HasErrors=true (expected false).
2. TestRepoVerifyCommand/resource_without_metadata — Creates no-meta-cmd (no .md). List() finds nothing -> HasWarnings=false (expected true).
3. TestRepoVerifyWithFix — Creates test-cmd (no .md). No resources found -> no fix performed -> GetMetadata returns nil -> meta.Name causes nil pointer dereference PANIC.
4. TestVerifyRepository_TypeMismatch — tracked separately as ai-config-manager-6al4 (same root cause).

## Fix

Update tests to create command files with .md extension and proper frontmatter, matching the pattern used in pkg/repo/manager_test.go. Example: test-cmd.md with YAML frontmatter containing name and description.

## Duplicates Closed

- ai-config-manager-lft2 (closed as duplicate)
- ai-config-manager-7co2 (closed as duplicate)
close_reason: Fixed: Updated test files to use .md extension and proper frontmatter

title: Integration tests for repair commands
description:
## Description

Create comprehensive integration tests that exercise the full repair workflow end-to-end, covering edge cases with packages, hierarchical commands, combined flags, and multi-tool scenarios.

## Context

The project has an established integration test infrastructure in `test/` using `runAimgr(t, args...)` for CLI-level testing with `AIMGR_REPO_PATH` env isolation. E2E tests in `test/e2e/` build a real binary. Package workflow tests exist in `test/package_test.go` and manifest tests in `test/ai_package_test.go` — follow these patterns.

Existing verify helpers (`test/verify_helpers.go`) provide `AssertVerifyClean()` and `AssertVerifyHasErrors()` — similar helpers should be created for repair.

## Instructions

1. Create `test/repair_test.go` with integration tests covering:

   **Basic repair scenarios:**
   - Repair with broken symlinks → symlinks get fixed
   - Repair with missing resources (in manifest but not on disk) → resources get installed
   - Repair with no issues → clean output, no changes
   - Repair with orphaned symlinks → hint printed, no auto-removal

   **Package scenarios:**
   - Manifest has `package/foo` → package members installed correctly
   - Manifest has `package/foo` but package doesn't exist in repo → proper error/warning
   - Package exists but one member resource is missing from repo → warning about repo issue

   **Hierarchical command scenarios:**
   - Broken symlink at `commands/namespace/command.md` → repaired correctly
   - Missing hierarchical command → installed with parent directory created
   - Unmanaged file at `commands/namespace/stale.md` → detected by --reset

   **--reset scenarios:**
   - Unmanaged regular files in resource dirs → detected and removable
   - Unmanaged files in namespace subdirectories → detected (one-level recursion)
   - Mix of managed symlinks and unmanaged files → only unmanaged removed
   - Symlinks to non-aimgr locations → treated as unmanaged
   - Root config files NOT touched (`.claude/settings.json` etc.)

   **--prune-package scenarios:**
   - Invalid individual resource ref in manifest → removed from manifest
   - Invalid package ref → removed from manifest
   - Valid refs → not touched
   - Mix of valid and invalid → only invalid removed

   **Combined flag scenarios:**
   - `--reset --prune-package` → both behaviors compose
   - `--reset --force` → no prompts, unmanaged files removed
   - `--dry-run` with all flag combinations → no filesystem changes

   **Multi-tool scenarios:**
   - Project with both Claude and OpenCode tools detected → both tool directories repaired

2. Create `test/repo_repair_test.go` with integration tests covering:
   - Missing metadata → created
   - Orphaned metadata → removed
   - Dry-run mode → no changes
   - No issues → clean output

3. Create `test/repair_helpers.go` with shared test helpers:
   - `AssertRepairClean(t, args...)` — runs repair, asserts no issues
   - `AssertRepairFixed(t, args..., expectedFixes)` — runs repair, asserts specific fixes applied
   - Helper to create "broken" project state (broken symlinks, unmanaged files, stale manifest entries)

## Files to Modify

- `test/repair_test.go` — CREATE: integration tests for `aimgr repair`
- `test/repo_repair_test.go` — CREATE: integration tests for `aimgr repo repair`
- `test/repair_helpers.go` — CREATE: shared test helpers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All scenarios listed above have corresponding test cases
- [ ] Tests use existing patterns: `t.TempDir()`, `AIMGR_REPO_PATH` env isolation, `runAimgr()`
- [ ] Package expansion is tested with real package JSON files
- [ ] Hierarchical commands tested with real namespace directory structures
- [ ] --force tests verify no interactive prompts are triggered (non-TTY safe)
- [ ] --dry-run tests verify zero filesystem mutations (snapshot before/after)
- [ ] Tests are independent and don't leak state between runs
- [ ] `go test ./test/ -run TestRepair -v` passes
- [ ] `go test ./test/ -run TestRepoRepair -v` passes
close_reason: 23 integration tests passing across repair and repo repair

title: Implement --prune-package flag for aimgr repair
description:
## Description

Implement the `--prune-package` flag on `aimgr repair` that removes invalid resource references from `ai.package.yaml` when the referenced resources don't exist in the repo. Includes a smart escalation flow that tries less destructive options first.

## Context

The `ai.package.yaml` manifest can get stale when resources are removed from the repo but not from the manifest. Today, `verify` reports these as `not-installed` issues but `--fix` fails to install them (resource not in repo). The `--prune-package` flag cleans this up.

The manifest is managed by `pkg/manifest/manifest.go` — it has `Load()`, `Save()`, `Add()`, `Remove()` methods. Resource references are strings like `"command/deploy"`, `"skill/pdf"`, `"package/my-pack"`.

## Instructions

1. In `cmd/repair.go`, implement `--prune-package` flag logic:
   - After standard repair, check each resource reference in `ai.package.yaml` against the repo
   - For individual resources (`command/x`, `skill/y`, `agent/z`): check if resource exists via `manager.Get()`
   - For package references (`package/foo`): check if package exists AND all its constituent resources exist via `manager.GetPackage()` + `manager.ValidatePackageResources()`

2. **Smart escalation flow** (interactive mode):
   For each invalid reference, offer options in this order:
   ```
   ⚠ skill:code-review not found in repo
   
   ? How to resolve:
     › Run repo sync first (repo sources may be outdated)
       Run repo repair first (repo metadata may be broken)
       Remove from ai.package.yaml
       Skip (do nothing)
   ```
   - If user picks "repo sync": run the equivalent of `aimgr repo sync`, then re-check. If still missing, re-prompt without the sync option.
   - If user picks "repo repair": run the equivalent of `aimgr repo repair`, then re-check. If still missing, re-prompt without that option.
   - If user picks "remove": call `manifest.Remove(ref)` then `manifest.Save()`
   - If user picks "skip": move to next invalid reference

3. **Interaction with --force**: Skip escalation entirely — go straight to removing invalid references from the manifest.

4. **Interaction with --dry-run**: List invalid references that WOULD be removed without modifying the file.

5. **Package edge case**: If `package/foo` exists in repo but one of its constituent resources is missing, this is a REPO issue not a manifest issue. Report it as a warning suggesting `aimgr repo repair` but do NOT remove the package from the manifest.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/repair.go` — UPDATE: implement --prune-package logic

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aimgr repair --prune-package` detects invalid manifest references
- [ ] Interactive escalation flow works: repo sync → repo repair → remove → skip
- [ ] After each escalation step, resource is re-checked before proceeding
- [ ] Individual resources (command/skill/agent) validated against repo
- [ ] Package references validated: package exists AND member resources exist
- [ ] Partial package (exists but missing members) triggers warning, not removal
- [ ] `--prune-package --force` removes invalid refs without prompting
- [ ] `--prune-package --dry-run` lists invalid refs without modifying manifest
- [ ] Manifest is properly saved after modifications (via manifest.Save())
- [ ] Unit tests cover: invalid individual ref, invalid package ref, partial package, escalation paths, force mode, dry-run mode, all-valid case (no-op)
- [ ] `go test ./cmd/ -run TestRepairPrunePackage` passes
close_reason: Implemented --prune-package flag with 9 tests passing

title: Implement --reset flag for aimgr repair
description:
## Description

Implement the `--reset` flag on `aimgr repair` that removes unmanaged files from resource directories, making them match the manifest exactly. This is new functionality — nothing like it exists today.

## Context

"Unmanaged files" are files in tool resource directories (`.claude/commands/`, `.opencode/skills/`, etc.) that are NOT symlinks pointing to the aimgr repo. Today, aimgr only touches files it manages (symlinks with target starting with repoPath). The --reset flag extends repair to also clean up everything else.

Currently, "managed by aimgr" is determined by: `os.ModeSymlink` check + `strings.HasPrefix(target, repoPath)`.

## Instructions

1. In `cmd/repair.go`, implement the `--reset` flag logic:
   - After running the standard repair, scan all resource directories for unmanaged files
   - A file is "unmanaged" if it is NOT a symlink pointing to the aimgr repo
   - For each unmanaged file/directory found, prompt the user interactively:
     - Show the file path
     - Options: "Remove all", "Keep all", "Choose individually" (follow pattern from `cmd/clean.go` confirmation flow)
   - Remove confirmed files via `os.Remove` (files) or `os.RemoveAll` (directories)

2. **Scope of scanning**: Only scan resource subdirectories, NOT root config files:
   - `.claude/commands/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.claude/agents/`
   - `.opencode/commands/`, `.opencode/skills/`, `.opencode/agents/`
   - Do NOT touch `.claude/settings.json`, `.opencode/config.yaml`, or other root-level files

3. **Package awareness**: When building the "managed" set, expand `package/foo` from the manifest to its constituent resources. A file installed by a package IS managed even though the manifest says `package/foo` not `command/bar`.

4. **Hierarchical commands**: When scanning `.claude/commands/namespace/`, recognize that `namespace/command.md` as a symlink to aimgr is managed. Non-symlink files in namespace directories are unmanaged. One-level recursion only (matching existing verify behavior).

5. **Interaction with --force**: When `--force` is set, skip all prompts and remove all unmanaged files. (The --force flag itself is defined in the scaffold task, this task implements the interaction.)

6. **Interaction with --dry-run**: When `--dry-run` is set, list unmanaged files that WOULD be removed without removing them.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/repair.go` — UPDATE: implement --reset logic

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aimgr repair --reset` lists unmanaged files and prompts for removal
- [ ] Only resource subdirectories are scanned (not root config files)
- [ ] Package member resources are correctly identified as "managed"
- [ ] Hierarchical command namespaces are scanned with one-level recursion
- [ ] `--reset --force` removes without prompting
- [ ] `--reset --dry-run` shows what would be removed without acting
- [ ] Unmanaged regular files are removed
- [ ] Unmanaged directories (with contents) are removed via os.RemoveAll
- [ ] Symlinks pointing to non-aimgr locations are treated as unmanaged
- [ ] Unit tests cover: unmanaged file detection, package expansion, nested commands, force mode, dry-run mode, mixed managed/unmanaged directories
- [ ] `go test ./cmd/ -run TestRepairReset` passes
close_reason: Implemented --reset flag with 9 tests passing

title: Fix golangci-lint failures in CI
description: CI fails on golangci-lint with 4 issues: (1) goconst: string ✗ has 6 occurrences in cmd/list_installed.go, (2) goconst: string orphaned has 6 occurrences in cmd/project_verify.go, (3) gocyclo: checkManifestSync has complexity 41 (>30) in cmd/project_verify.go, (4) staticcheck: empty branch SA9003 in pkg/repo/remove_test.go. These cause the Test job to fail, blocking Build and E2E jobs.
close_reason: Fixed all 4 golangci-lint issues: extracted statusIconFail constant (goconst), extracted issue type constants (goconst), refactored checkManifestSync into smaller functions (gocyclo), fixed empty branch in remove_test.go (staticcheck SA9003)

description:

### 2. Write and publish release notes

```bash
## What's Changed

### Fixed

- `verify` scans subdirectories for nested commands and detects orphaned resources
- Improved list legend labels and shortened broken status display

- Corrected 'aimgr project verify' references to 'aimgr verify' in sync/remove warnings

## Full Changelog
https://github.com/hk9890/ai-config-manager/compare/v2.7.0...v2.8.0
NOTES

gh release edit v2.8.0 --notes-file /tmp/release-notes.md
```


## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Full Changelog comparison link included
- [ ] Notes published to GitHub release

title: verify doesn't detect orphaned resources (installed but not in manifest)
description:
## Description

`aimgr verify` does not detect resources that are installed on disk but not declared in `ai.package.yaml`. These show up as `*` (Not in manifest) in `aimgr list` but are completely invisible to `aimgr verify`.

The `list` command correctly flags these resources and the broken count message tells users to run `aimgr verify --fix`, but verify doesn't know about them and can't fix them.

## Root Cause

`checkManifestSync()` in `cmd/project_verify.go` (lines 269-435) only checks in one direction: **manifest → disk** ("are manifest entries installed?"). It iterates over `mf.Resources` and checks if each one exists on disk.

It never checks **disk → manifest** ("are installed resources in the manifest?"). This means orphaned installations are invisible.

Notably, the `VerifyIssue` struct already has an `"orphaned"` issue type in its comment (line 123), and `fixVerifyIssues` has a `case "orphaned":` handler (line 608), but **no code ever creates issues with `IssueType: "orphaned"`**. This is dead/stub code — the orphan detection was planned but never implemented.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have a resource installed that is NOT in `ai.package.yaml` (e.g., manually installed or left over after manifest edit)
2. Run `aimgr list` — shows `*` in the Sync column for the resource
3. Run `aimgr verify` — does not report the orphaned resource

## Expected Behavior

`aimgr verify` should report orphaned resources (installed but not in manifest) as warnings, consistent with how `aimgr list` shows them as `*`.

## Instructions

1. In `checkManifestSync()` in `cmd/project_verify.go`, after checking manifest → disk, add a **disk → manifest** check:
   - Scan all tool directories for installed symlinks (similar to how `verifyDirectory` or `installer.List` does)
   - For each installed resource, check if it appears in the expanded manifest set
   - If not, create a `VerifyIssue` with `IssueType: "orphaned"`
2. The `VerifyIssue` struct already supports `"orphaned"` in its comment — make it real
3. The `fixVerifyIssues` function already has `case "orphaned":` (line 608) — verify it handles orphans correctly (likely should offer to uninstall or add to manifest)
4. Display orphaned resources as warnings (not errors) since they're not broken, just unmanaged
5. Consider: should `--fix` for orphans uninstall the resource or add it to manifest? Probably just report and let user decide.

## Files to Modify

- `cmd/project_verify.go` — Add disk → manifest orphan detection in `checkManifestSync()`
- `cmd/project_verify.go` — Verify the `case "orphaned":` handler in `fixVerifyIssues` is correct
- `cmd/project_verify_test.go` — Add tests for orphan detection

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aimgr verify` reports resources installed but not in manifest as "orphaned" warnings
- [ ] Output clearly identifies which resources are orphaned and which tool they're installed for
- [ ] The `"orphaned"` issue type is now actually used (no more dead code)
- [ ] `aimgr verify --fix` handles orphaned resources (at minimum: reports them; ideally: offers action)
- [ ] Existing verify tests still pass
- [ ] New test covers orphan detection scenario
- [ ] No false positives when manifest doesn't exist (projects without ai.package.yaml)
close_reason: Implemented disk→manifest orphan detection in checkManifestSync(). Added findOrphanedResources() helper that scans tool directories for installed symlinks not in the expanded manifest (including package member expansion). Orphans are reported as warnings with IssueType 'orphaned'. The existing fixVerifyIssues 'orphaned' case now provides actionable type/name hints. Added 14 new test cases covering: orphaned skills, commands, nested commands, deduplication across tools, package member exclusion, no false positives without manifest, and ignoring non-symlinks. All tests pass.

title: list legend uses confusing jargon: replace 'manifest' with 'ai.package.yaml'
description:
## Description
The \`aimgr list\` legend shows:
\`\`\`
Legend:
  ✓ = In sync  * = Not in manifest  ⚠ = Not installed  - = No manifest
\`\`\`

"manifest" is developer jargon. Users don't know what "manifest" refers to. It should say \`ai.package.yaml\` directly — that's the actual file name and makes it immediately clear.

## Fix
Update the legend at \`cmd/list_installed.go\` line 431 from:
\`\`\`
✓ = In sync  * = Not in manifest  ⚠ = Not installed  - = No manifest
\`\`\`
To:
\`\`\`
✓ = In sync  * = Not in ai.package.yaml  ⚠ = Not installed  - = No ai.package.yaml
\`\`\`

Also update the long description at lines 56-59 for consistency.

## Files to Modify
- \`cmd/list_installed.go\` — line 431: update legend text
- \`cmd/list_installed.go\` — lines 56-59: update long description

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Legend says \`ai.package.yaml\` instead of \`manifest\`
- [ ] "Not installed" stays as-is
- [ ] Long description updated to match
- [ ] \`make test\` passes
close_reason: Fixed: legend and long description now say ai.package.yaml instead of manifest. Tests updated. make test passes.

title: list status column shows redundant text after symbols — just use ✗ alone
description:
## Description
In \`aimgr list\`, the status column shows \`✗ broken\` which gets truncated to \`✗ br…\` because the column is too narrow. But the real fix is simpler: the word "broken" is redundant — just show \`✗\`.

The \`✓\` column already works with just the symbol (no "ok" text). \`✗\` should be the same.

## Root Cause
Status text at \`cmd/list_installed.go\` lines 383-386:
\`\`\`go
status := statusIconOK  // "✓"
if cmd.Health == string(resource.HealthBroken) {
    status = "✗ broken"
}
\`\`\`

Should just be \`"✗"\` — no text needed.

## Files to Modify
- \`cmd/list_installed.go\` — lines 383-386: change \`"✗ broken"\` to \`"✗"\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status column shows just \`✗\` for broken resources (no text)
- [ ] No truncation issue
- [ ] \`make test\` passes
close_reason: Fixed: changed ✗ broken to ✗ in all 3 places in list_installed.go. make test passes.

title: TestListEnhanced tests fail: expect targets/sync_status fields not yet in list output
close_reason: Tests now call listInstalledCmd instead of listCmd. Also fixed JSON/YAML parsing to expect plain arrays and updated uninstalled-resource tests to match listInstalledCmd behavior. All 8 TestListEnhanced tests pass, full suite passes.
